questions.stem: Under ASC 250, a change in accounting principle (e.g., from FIFO to weighted average) is generally accounted for by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospective application only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospective application to all prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative effect adjustment to current-period income

choices.label: D
choices.text: A prior period adjustment charged to additional paid-in capital

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-5 requires that a change in accounting principle be reported through retrospective application. The entity adjusts the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities at the beginning of the first period presented, with a corresponding offset to the opening balance of retained earnings. All prior periods presented are restated as if the new principle had always been used.
questions.explanationJa: 会計方針の変更は遡及適用（retrospective application）により処理する。表示される最初の期間の期首時点で資産・負債の帳簿価額を調整し、対応額を利益剰余金の期首残高に反映する。表示される全ての過去の期間を、新方針が常に使用されていたかのように修正再表示する。

questions.stem: A company revises the estimated useful life of equipment from 10 years to 7 years after using it for 4 years. This change is accounted for:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively, restating prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively, with the new depreciation rate applied to the remaining book value over the remaining useful life

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an error correction

choices.label: D
choices.text: By recording a cumulative adjustment to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-17, a change in accounting estimate is accounted for prospectively. The remaining depreciable amount (book value less salvage) is depreciated over the new remaining useful life (7 - 4 = 3 years). No restatement of prior periods is made because estimates are inherently subject to revision.
questions.explanationJa: 見積りの変更は将来に向かって（prospectively）処理する。残存償却可能額（帳簿価額 - 残存価額）を新しい残存耐用年数（7 - 4 = 3年）で償却する。見積りは本質的に修正されうるものであるため、過去の修正再表示は行わない。

questions.stem: An entity discovers that depreciation expense was understated by $50,000 in a prior year. This is a material error. How should it be corrected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record additional depreciation in the current year

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the prior period financial statements (prior period adjustment to retained earnings)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disclose the error in notes only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record a cumulative effect adjustment in current year income

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-23 requires that material errors in previously issued financial statements be corrected by restating the prior period financial statements. The correction is reported as a prior period adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings in the earliest period presented. Comparative financial statements are revised.
questions.explanationJa: 重要な誤謬は過去の期間の財務諸表を修正再表示（restatement）して訂正する。訂正額は表示される最も早い期間の利益剰余金期首残高に対する前期修正（prior period adjustment）として報告する。比較財務諸表も修正する。

questions.stem: A change in reporting entity (such as presenting consolidated statements for the first time) requires:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospective treatment only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospective application—all prior periods must be restated to show the new reporting entity

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative effect adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: No accounting change; disclosure only

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-21 requires that a change in the reporting entity be applied retrospectively. Financial statements of all prior periods presented must be restated to reflect the financial statements of the new reporting entity. This ensures comparability across periods presented.
questions.explanationJa: 報告主体の変更は遡及適用する。表示される全ての過去の期間の財務諸表を新しい報告主体に基づき修正再表示し、期間間の比較可能性を確保する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, when is it acceptable to NOT apply retrospective treatment for a change in accounting principle?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the change results in a decrease in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: When retrospective application is impracticable

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the change involves inventory methods

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the auditors recommend against it

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-7 provides an impracticability exception. Retrospective application is impracticable when: (a) the effects cannot be determined despite reasonable effort, (b) the entity cannot determine assumptions about management's intent, or (c) significant estimates are required that cannot be objectively verified. In such cases, the change is applied prospectively from the earliest practicable date.
questions.explanationJa: 遡及適用が実務上不可能（impracticable）となるのは：(a) 合理的な努力でも影響額を決定できない、(b) 経営者の意図に関する仮定を決定できない、(c) 客観的に検証不能な重要見積りが必要な場合。この場合、実務上可能な最も早い日から将来に向かって適用する。

questions.stem: A change from straight-line to double-declining balance depreciation is treated as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in accounting principle requiring retrospective application

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle (prospective)

choices.label: C
choices.text: An error correction

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in reporting entity

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-18 states that a change in depreciation method is treated as a change in estimate effected by a change in accounting principle. It is accounted for prospectively (like a change in estimate) because depreciation methods are a means of recognizing asset cost over its useful life—inseparable from the estimate of useful life and residual value.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却方法の変更は「会計方針の変更を伴う見積りの変更」として取り扱う。減価償却方法は耐用年数にわたる資産原価の認識手段であり、耐用年数・残存価額の見積りと不可分なため、将来に向かって（prospectively）処理する。

questions.stem: When a change in accounting principle is made, which of the following disclosures is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the name of the new accounting principle

choices.label: B
choices.text: The nature of and reason for the change, the method of applying the change, and the effect on income from continuing operations and net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: A statement that the auditor approved the change

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosure is not required if the change increases net income

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-50-1 requires disclosure of: the nature and reason for the change (including why the new principle is preferable), the method of applying the change (retrospective or prospective if impracticable), the effect on income from continuing operations, net income, EPS, and any other affected line items for all periods presented.
questions.explanationJa: 会計方針変更時の開示事項：変更の性質と理由（新方針が望ましい理由含む）、適用方法（遡及or将来）、継続事業利益・当期純利益・EPSへの影響、表示される全期間の影響を受ける項目。

questions.stem: An error in a previously issued interim financial statement of the current fiscal year is discovered in a subsequent interim period. How should it be corrected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Correct it in the current interim period by including the cumulative effect

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the previously issued interim period if it will be reissued

choices.label: C
choices.text: Wait until year-end to correct

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose in notes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-25, if comparative interim financial statements are being presented, the prior interim period's financial statements should be restated to correct the error. If prior interim statements will not be reissued, the correction is included in the determination of net income for the current interim period with disclosure.
questions.explanationJa: 比較中間財務諸表を表示する場合、過去の中間期間の財務諸表を修正再表示して誤謬を訂正する。過去の中間財務諸表を再発行しない場合は、開示を伴い当期中間期間の純利益算定に訂正を含める。

questions.stem: When a company changes from LIFO to FIFO, the retrospective application requirement means:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Inventory is restated to FIFO for all periods presented and opening retained earnings is adjusted

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the current year uses FIFO; prior years remain as originally reported

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative catch-up is recorded in the current year's income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The change is treated prospectively because LIFO data from prior years is unavailable

questions.explanation: A change from LIFO to FIFO is a change in accounting principle accounted for retrospectively under ASC 250-10-45-5. All prior periods presented must be restated as if FIFO had always been used. Opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented is adjusted for the cumulative effect. If retrospective application is impracticable, the earliest practicable date is used.
questions.explanationJa: LIFO→FIFOの変更は遡及適用される会計方針変更である。全ての表示期間をFIFOが常に使用されていたかのように修正再表示し、最も早い表示期間の利益剰余金期首残高を累積影響額で調整する。遡及適用が実務上不可能な場合は可能な最も早い日から適用する。

questions.stem: Ending inventory in Year 1 was overstated by $20,000. If this error is not corrected, what is the effect on Year 2's net income (assuming beginning inventory Year 2 is ending inventory Year 1)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Year 2 net income is understated by $20,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Year 2 net income is overstated by $20,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Year 2 net income is not affected

choices.label: D
choices.text: Year 2 net income is understated by $40,000

questions.explanation: An overstatement of ending inventory in Year 1 becomes an overstatement of beginning inventory in Year 2. Overstated beginning inventory leads to overstated cost of goods sold in Year 2, which understates Year 2 gross profit and net income by $20,000. This is a counterbalancing error—over two years the net effect on retained earnings is zero.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1期末棚卸の過大計上はYear 2期首棚卸の過大計上となる。期首棚卸が過大→Year 2のCOGS過大→Year 2純利益$20,000過少。これは相殺誤謬（counterbalancing error）であり、2年間を通じた利益剰余金への純影響はゼロである。

questions.stem: A company changes from FIFO to weighted average on January 1, Year 3. The following data is available: FIFO inventory at 12/31/Year 1 was $500,000, weighted average would have been $420,000; FIFO inventory at 12/31/Year 2 was $600,000, weighted average would have been $510,000. The tax rate is 25%. What is the cumulative effect adjustment to beginning retained earnings at 1/1/Year 2 (the earliest period presented)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $60,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $80,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $67,500 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: $90,000 decrease

questions.explanation: The cumulative difference at 12/31/Year 1 (beginning of Year 2) = FIFO − WA = $500,000 − $420,000 = $80,000 pre-tax. Under WA, inventory is lower, so retained earnings would have been lower. After-tax adjustment = $80,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $80,000 × 0.75 = $60,000 decrease to opening retained earnings.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1末（Year 2期首）時点の累積差額 = FIFO - 加重平均 = $500,000 - $420,000 = $80,000（税引前）。加重平均では棚卸資産が少ないため利益剰余金も少なくなる。税引後調整 = $80,000 ×（1 - 0.25）= $60,000の利益剰余金減少。

questions.stem: Equipment was purchased for $500,000 with a $50,000 salvage value and a 10-year useful life (straight-line). After 6 years, the company revises the total useful life to 8 years and salvage to $0. What is the revised annual depreciation expense?
questions.explanation: Original annual depreciation = ($500,000 − $50,000) / 10 = $45,000. Accumulated after 6 years = $45,000 × 6 = $270,000. Book value at revision = $500,000 − $270,000 = $230,000. Remaining life = 8 − 6 = 2 years. New annual depreciation = ($230,000 − $0) / 2 = $230,000 / 2 = $115,000.

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 1, a company purchased equipment for $200,000 (10-year life, no salvage) but erroneously expensed the entire amount. The error is discovered at the end of Year 3. What is the adjustment to beginning retained earnings at January 1, Year 3 (ignoring taxes)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase of $160,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $200,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $140,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase of $180,000

questions.explanation: The equipment should have been capitalized at $200,000 and depreciated at $20,000/year. Through Year 2 (2 years of depreciation): Correct book value = $200,000 − ($20,000 × 2) = $160,000. As reported: fully expensed in Year 1, BV = $0. The error understated assets and retained earnings by $160,000 at 1/1/Year 3. Adjustment: increase retained earnings by $160,000.
questions.explanationJa: 設備は$200,000で資産計上し年$20,000償却すべきであった。Year 2末の正しいBV = $200,000 -（$20,000 × 2）= $160,000。報告上はYear 1で全額費用処理しBV = $0。1/1/Year 3時点で資産と利益剰余金が$160,000過少。調整：利益剰余金$160,000増加。

questions.stem: A company failed to accrue $30,000 of wages payable at December 31, Year 1. The wages were paid in January Year 2. If the error is not corrected, what is the combined effect on Year 1 and Year 2 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Year 1 overstated $30,000; Year 2 understated $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Year 1 understated $30,000; Year 2 overstated $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both years overstated by $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Year 1 overstated $30,000; Year 2 unaffected

questions.explanation: Failure to accrue wages at Year 1 end: expenses understated → Year 1 income OVERSTATED by $30,000. When paid in Year 2, the full $30,000 hits Year 2 expense (including the $30,000 that should have been Year 1 expense): Year 2 expenses overstated → Year 2 income UNDERSTATED by $30,000. This is a counterbalancing error; by end of Year 2, retained earnings are correct.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1末の給与未払計上漏れ：費用過少→Year 1利益$30,000過大。Year 2支払時に$30,000全額がYear 2費用に計上（Year 1分含む）：Year 2費用過大→Year 2利益$30,000過少。相殺誤謬であり、Year 2末の利益剰余金は正しくなる。

questions.stem: A company changed from FIFO to LIFO on January 1, Year 3. For Year 2, FIFO COGS was $800,000 and LIFO COGS would have been $860,000. The tax rate is 30% and there are 100,000 shares outstanding. What is the per-share effect of the restatement on Year 2's basic EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $0.42

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $0.60

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $0.42

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $0.18

questions.explanation: Under LIFO, COGS is higher by $60,000 ($860,000 − $800,000), so pre-tax income decreases by $60,000. After-tax effect = $60,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $42,000 decrease in net income. EPS effect = $42,000 / 100,000 shares = $0.42 decrease per share.

questions.stem: In Year 4, a company discovers it failed to record $75,000 of depreciation in Year 2 and $75,000 in Year 3 (total understatement $150,000). The tax rate is 20%. The company presents Years 3 and 4 comparatively. What is the prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings of Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $60,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $75,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $120,000 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: $150,000 decrease

questions.explanation: The earliest period presented is Year 3. The error prior to Year 3 (i.e., Year 2's $75,000 understatement) affects opening retained earnings of Year 3. Pre-tax adjustment = $75,000 (understated depreciation → overstated retained earnings). After-tax = $75,000 × (1 − 0.20) = $60,000 decrease to 1/1/Year 3 retained earnings. Year 3's $75,000 error is corrected by restating Year 3's depreciation expense.
questions.explanationJa: 表示される最も早い期間はYear 3。Year 2の$75,000償却費過少はYear 3期首利益剰余金に影響する。税引前調整 = $75,000（償却費過少→RE過大）。税引後 = $75,000 ×（1 - 0.20）= $60,000のRE減少。Year 3の$75,000誤謬はYear 3の償却費を修正再表示して訂正する。

questions.stem: A company estimated warranty costs at 2% of sales in Years 1-3. In Year 4, based on new data, the estimate is revised to 3% of sales. Year 4 sales are $5,000,000. What warranty expense does the company report in Year 4?
questions.explanation: A change in estimate is applied prospectively. The new 3% rate applies only to Year 4 and forward. Year 4 warranty expense = $5,000,000 × 3% = $150,000. No catch-up adjustment is made for prior years' estimates. Prior years remain as reported (at 2%).
questions.explanationJa: 見積りの変更は将来に向かって適用する。新しい3%の率はYear 4以降にのみ適用。Year 4保証費用 = $5,000,000 × 3% = $150,000。過年度のキャッチアップ調整は行わない。過年度は2%のまま。

questions.stem: Ending inventory was understated by $40,000 in Year 1 and overstated by $25,000 in Year 2. If neither error was corrected, what is the net effect on Year 3's beginning retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $25,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Understated by $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $25,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect (errors fully counterbalanced)

questions.explanation: Year 1 ending inventory understated $40,000: Year 1 income understated $40,000, Year 2 income overstated $40,000 (counterbalancing). Year 2 ending inventory overstated $25,000: Year 2 income overstated $25,000. Combined effect on Year 2 income: +$40,000 + $25,000 = +$65,000 overstated. Cumulative through Year 2: Year 1 effect on RE (−$40,000) + Year 2 excess (+$65,000) = +$25,000 overstated. Year 3 beginning RE is overstated by $25,000.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1期末棚卸$40,000過少：Year 1利益$40,000過少、Year 2利益$40,000過大（相殺）。Year 2期末棚卸$25,000過大：Year 2利益$25,000過大。Year 2利益合計：+$40,000 + $25,000 = +$65,000過大。累積RE影響：Year 1（-$40,000）+ Year 2（+$65,000）= +$25,000過大。Year 3期首REは$25,000過大。

questions.stem: A construction company changes from the completed-contract method to the percentage-of-completion method. It is impracticable to determine the cumulative effect for projects completed in prior years. Under ASC 250, how is this change applied?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The change cannot be made

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply prospectively from the earliest date practicable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record an estimated cumulative adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Apply retrospectively using management's best guess

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-9, when it is impracticable to determine the period-specific or cumulative effects of a change in principle, the entity applies the new principle prospectively from the earliest date practicable. The entity must disclose why retrospective application was impracticable.
questions.explanationJa: 方針変更の各期間別・累積的影響の算定が実務上不可能な場合、実務上可能な最も早い日から新方針を将来に向かって適用する。遡及適用が不可能であった理由の開示が必要である。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a company paid $120,000 for a 3-year insurance policy and expensed the entire amount in Year 1. The error is discovered on December 31, Year 2. Ignoring taxes, what is the correcting journal entry at December 31, Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Prepaid Insurance $40,000; Credit Retained Earnings $40,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Prepaid Insurance $40,000; Credit Retained Earnings $80,000; Debit Insurance Expense $40,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Prepaid Insurance $40,000; Credit Insurance Expense $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Prepaid Insurance $80,000; Credit Retained Earnings $80,000

questions.explanation: The policy covers Years 1, 2, 3 at $40,000/year. In Year 1, $120,000 was expensed instead of $40,000 (overstatement of $80,000). Year 2's $40,000 expense was never recorded. At 12/31/Year 2: Prepaid remaining = $40,000 (for Year 3). Retained earnings effect: Year 1 was overstated by $80,000 expense; Year 2 was understated by $40,000 expense (not recorded). Net prior period impact = $80,000 − $40,000 = $40,000 understatement of RE. Entry: Dr Prepaid Insurance $40,000 (Year 3 coverage), Cr Retained Earnings $40,000.
questions.explanationJa: 保険はYear 1-3の各$40,000。Year 1で$120,000全額費用処理（$80,000過大）。Year 2の$40,000費用は未計上。12/31/Year 2時点の前払残高 = $40,000（Year 3分）。RE影響：Year 1費用$80,000過大、Year 2費用$40,000過少。純影響 = $80,000 - $40,000 = RE $40,000過少。仕訳：Dr 前払保険$40,000 / Cr 利益剰余金$40,000。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate Effected by Change in Principle
questions.stem: A company changes its depreciation method from double-declining balance to straight-line because the new method better reflects the pattern of economic benefits. Under ASC 250, how is this change reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively with restatement of prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively as a change in estimate effected by a change in principle

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an error correction with a prior period adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cumulative effect adjustment to beginning retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-18, a change in depreciation method is treated as a change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle. It is applied prospectively — the remaining book value is depreciated using the new method over the remaining useful life. No retrospective adjustment is required.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-18に基づき、減価償却方法の変更は「会計方針の変更を伴う見積りの変更」として扱われる。将来に向かって適用 — 残存帳簿価額を新方法で残存耐用年数にわたり償却する。遡及修正は不要。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, what justification is required for a voluntary change in accounting principle?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The change must be mandated by a new accounting standard

choices.label: B
choices.text: The entity must demonstrate that the new principle is preferable

choices.label: C
choices.text: The change must reduce income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: No justification is needed for voluntary changes

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-2, a voluntary change in accounting principle is permitted only if the entity can justify that the newly adopted principle is preferable. The entity must explain why the new principle provides more reliable and relevant financial information. An auditor's letter acknowledging preferability is typically required.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-2に基づき、任意の会計方針の変更は、新たに採用する方針がより望ましい（preferable）ことを正当化できる場合にのみ認められる。新方針がより信頼性と適合性のある財務情報を提供する理由を説明する必要がある。通常、監査人による望ましさの確認書が求められる。

questions.stem: A company discovers that it has been using the cash basis instead of the accrual basis. At the beginning of Year 3, accounts receivable should be $80,000, accounts payable should be $50,000, and prepaid insurance should be $12,000. Ignoring taxes, what is the correcting entry to retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $42,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $42,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $92,000

questions.explanation: To convert from cash to accrual at the beginning of Year 3: Debit Accounts Receivable $80,000 (increases income), Debit Prepaid Insurance $12,000 (increases assets), Credit Accounts Payable $50,000 (increases expense). Net effect on retained earnings = $80,000 + $12,000 − $50,000 = $42,000 credit (increase).
questions.explanationJa: Year 3期首に現金主義から発生主義への修正：Dr 売掛金 $80,000（収益増）、Dr 前払保険 $12,000（資産増）、Cr 買掛金 $50,000（費用増）。利益剰余金への純影響 = $80,000 + $12,000 − $50,000 = $42,000の貸方（増加）。

questions.stem: A company changes from LIFO to FIFO in Year 3 and presents two years of comparative statements (Years 2 and 3). The cumulative effect at the beginning of Year 2 is a $60,000 increase to inventory. What adjustment is made to the Year 2 comparative financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No adjustment to Year 2; only Year 3 is affected

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate Year 2 beginning retained earnings by $60,000 and recompute Year 2 under FIFO

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record $60,000 as a Year 3 gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose in notes only without restating

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250, a change in accounting principle (LIFO to FIFO) requires retrospective application. When presenting comparative statements, Year 2 is restated as if FIFO had always been used. The cumulative effect as of the beginning of Year 2 ($60,000) is adjusted to Year 2's beginning retained earnings. Year 2's income statement is recomputed under FIFO.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250に基づき、会計方針の変更（LIFOからFIFO）は遡及適用を要する。比較財務諸表ではYear 2をFIFOを常に使用していたかのように修正再表示する。Year 2期首時点の累積影響（$60,000）はYear 2の期首利益剰余金に調整し、Year 2の損益計算書をFIFOで再計算する。

questions.stem: Which of the following is an example of a change in reporting entity under ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Changing the depreciation method for all fixed assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: Presenting consolidated financial statements for the first time when a subsidiary was previously unconsolidated

choices.label: C
choices.text: Correcting an error in revenue recognition

choices.label: D
choices.text: Adopting a new FASB standard

questions.explanation: A change in reporting entity occurs when financial statements are, in effect, the statements of a different entity. Examples include presenting consolidated statements for the first time, or changing the specific subsidiaries included. This is reported by retrospectively adjusting all prior-period financial statements presented.
questions.explanationJa: 報告単位の変更は、財務諸表が実質的に異なる単位の財務諸表となる場合に生じる。例として、初めて連結財務諸表を作成する場合や含める子会社の変更がある。表示する全ての過年度財務諸表を遡及修正して報告する。

questions.stem: Year 1 ending inventory was overstated by $30,000. The error was NOT corrected. What is the impact on Year 3 financial statements (assuming no other errors)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Year 3 net income is overstated by $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Year 3 net income is understated by $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Year 3 net income is unaffected; Year 3 beginning retained earnings is unaffected

choices.label: D
choices.text: Year 3 net income is unaffected; Year 3 beginning retained earnings is overstated

questions.explanation: An ending inventory error self-corrects over two periods. Year 1 overstatement causes Year 1 income overstatement and Year 2 income understatement (same $30,000). By the beginning of Year 3, the error has fully counterbalanced — both income and retained earnings are correct. Year 3 financial statements are completely unaffected.
questions.explanationJa: 期末在庫の誤りは2期間で自動修正される。Year 1の過大計上はYear 1の利益過大とYear 2の利益過少（同額$30,000）を引き起こす。Year 3期首までに誤りは完全に相殺され、利益も利益剰余金も正しい。Year 3の財務諸表は全く影響を受けない。

questions.stem: In Year 1, a company incorrectly recognized $200,000 of revenue for goods shipped FOB destination that had not yet been received by the customer. The error is discovered in Year 2 after Year 1 statements were issued. How should this be corrected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reduce Year 2 revenue by $200,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate Year 1 financial statements as a prior period adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record a $200,000 expense in Year 2

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose in notes only

questions.explanation: Incorrectly recognizing revenue is an error, not a change in estimate or principle. Under ASC 250-10-45-23, errors in previously issued financial statements are corrected by restating the prior-period financial statements. The beginning retained earnings of Year 2 is adjusted, and Year 1 comparative statements are restated if presented.
questions.explanationJa: 収益の誤認識は誤謬であり、見積りや方針の変更ではない。ASC 250-10-45-23に基づき、発行済財務諸表の誤りは過年度財務諸表の修正再表示により訂正する。Year 2の期首利益剰余金を調整し、比較表示するYear 1財務諸表も修正再表示する。

questions.stem: A company has been amortizing a patent over 20 years. In Year 5, the company determines the remaining useful life is 5 years (not the original remaining 15 years). How is this change reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively restate all prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively — amortize the remaining carrying amount over 5 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: Immediately write off the patent

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record a cumulative effect adjustment to beginning retained earnings

questions.explanation: A change in useful life (amortization period) is a change in accounting estimate under ASC 250. Changes in estimates are applied prospectively. The remaining unamortized cost at the beginning of Year 5 is amortized over the revised remaining useful life of 5 years. No restatement of prior periods is required.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数（償却期間）の変更はASC 250に基づく見積りの変更。見積りの変更は将来に向かって適用する。Year 5期首の未償却残高を改訂後の残存耐用年数5年で償却する。過年度の修正再表示は不要。

questions.subtopic: Error in Tax Provision - Prior Year
questions.stem: A company discovers in Year 2 that it failed to record $75,000 of income tax expense in Year 1 (Year 1 statements have been issued). The tax rate is 25%. What is the correcting entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Income Tax Expense $75,000; Credit Income Tax Payable $75,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $75,000; Credit Income Tax Payable $75,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Income Tax Expense $75,000; Credit Retained Earnings $75,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Retained Earnings $75,000; Credit Cash $75,000

questions.explanation: Since the error is in previously issued financial statements, it is corrected as a prior period adjustment. The debit goes to Retained Earnings (not current year expense) to correct the overstatement of Year 1 income. The credit goes to Income Tax Payable (the liability that should have been recorded). If comparative Year 1 statements are presented, they are restated.
questions.explanationJa: 発行済財務諸表の誤りは修正再表示として訂正する。Year 1の利益過大を修正するため利益剰余金を借方（当期費用ではない）に記入。Year 1に計上すべきだった未払法人税を貸方に記入。比較表示するYear 1財務諸表は修正再表示する。

questions.stem: A company changes from weighted average to FIFO inventory method at the beginning of Year 3. The pre-tax cumulative effect at January 1, Year 3 is an increase to inventory of $90,000. The tax rate is 30%. What is the adjustment to beginning retained earnings of Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase of $90,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $63,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $27,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $63,000

questions.explanation: Retrospective application adjusts the beginning retained earnings of the earliest period presented. The pre-tax effect is a $90,000 increase to inventory (and thus income). After-tax impact = $90,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $63,000 increase to retained earnings. The remaining $27,000 increases deferred tax liability.
questions.explanationJa: 遡及適用は表示する最も早い期間の期首利益剰余金を調整する。税引前影響は在庫（および利益）の$90,000増加。税引後影響 = $90,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $63,000の利益剰余金増加。残り$27,000は繰延税金負債の増加。

questions.subtopic: Change from FIFO to Average Cost
questions.stem: A company voluntarily changes from FIFO to average cost for inventory. Under ASC 250, how is this change reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospectively as a change in estimate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospectively with restatement of all prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: Current period cumulative effect adjustment only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retrospectively, but only if mandated by FASB

questions.explanation: A change from FIFO to average cost is a voluntary change in accounting principle. Under ASC 250-10-45-5, changes in accounting principle are applied retrospectively to all prior periods presented, unless it is impracticable to do so. The entity must demonstrate that average cost is the preferable method.
questions.explanationJa: FIFOから平均原価法への変更は任意の会計方針の変更。ASC 250-10-45-5に基づき、会計方針の変更は実務上不可能でない限り、表示する全ての過年度に遡及適用する。平均原価法がより望ましい方法であることの正当化が必要。

questions.stem: In the second quarter, a company revises its estimate of annual warranty expense from 2% to 3% of sales. Year-to-date sales through Q2 are $4,000,000 and Q1 warranty expense was $40,000 (at 2% of $2,000,000 Q1 sales). What warranty expense should be reported in Q2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 270, a change in estimate during an interim period is accounted for in the period of change. Year-to-date warranty expense at new rate = $4,000,000 × 3% = $120,000. Less Q1 expense already recorded = $40,000. Q2 expense = $120,000 − $40,000 = $80,000. The catch-up for Q1 understatement is included in Q2.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 270に基づき、中間期の見積り変更はその変更期間に処理する。年度累計保証費用（新率）= $4,000,000 × 3% = $120,000。Q1計上済 = $40,000を控除。Q2費用 = $120,000 − $40,000 = $80,000。Q1の過少計上分のキャッチアップがQ2に含まれる。

questions.subtopic: Error Detection After Financial Statement Issuance
questions.stem: After Year 1 financial statements are issued, a material error is discovered. Under ASC 250, how is this error classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a change in accounting estimate

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a change in accounting principle

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment requiring restatement

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a prospective adjustment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-23, an error in previously issued financial statements is corrected by restating the financial statements for each prior period presented that is affected (prior period adjustment). The opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented is also adjusted for the cumulative effect of the error on periods not presented.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-23に基づき、発行済財務諸表の誤りは影響を受ける表示済各期間の財務諸表を修正再表示して訂正する（修正再表示）。表示されていない期間に対する累積的影響は、表示する最も早い期間の期首利益剰余金に調整する。

questions.stem: In Year 3, a court rules on a lawsuit that originated in Year 1. The company had recorded no liability. The settlement of $500,000 relates entirely to Year 1 events. How should this be reported in Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment to Year 1 retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a Year 3 expense (loss from litigation)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Allocated between Years 1, 2, and 3

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a change in accounting estimate

questions.explanation: A litigation settlement is generally recognized in the period it is resolved (Year 3) as an expense or loss, not as a prior period adjustment. Prior period adjustments under GAAP are limited to corrections of errors in previously issued financial statements. A contingent liability that was not previously recognizable (did not meet the 'probable and estimable' criteria) is not an error — it is a current-period event.
questions.explanationJa: 訴訟和解金は通常、解決した期間（Year 3）に費用・損失として認識し、修正再表示とはしない。GAAPの修正再表示は発行済財務諸表の誤りの訂正に限定される。以前認識要件を満たさなかった偶発債務（「発生可能性が高く合理的に見積可能」基準未達）は誤りではなく当期の事象である。

questions.subtopic: Change in Entity - Equity Method to Consolidation
questions.stem: A company increases its ownership of an investee from 30% to 60%, gaining control. How should the change from the equity method to consolidation be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively as a change in reporting entity

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively from the date control is obtained

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an error correction

choices.label: D
choices.text: No change in accounting is required

questions.explanation: When an investor obtains control of an investee, it transitions from the equity method to consolidation. Under ASC 810 and ASC 805, this is treated as a business combination achieved in stages (step acquisition). The change is applied prospectively from the acquisition date — the previously held equity interest is remeasured to fair value, and any gain or loss is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 投資者が被投資会社の支配を獲得した場合、持分法から連結に移行する。ASC 810およびASC 805に基づき、段階的取得（ステップアクイジション）として処理する。変更は取得日から将来に向かって適用 — 以前保有していた持分を公正価値で再測定し、損益を認識する。

questions.stem: When an entity restates previously issued financial statements to correct an error, which of the following disclosures is required under ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The nature of the error only

choices.label: B
choices.text: The nature of the error, the effect on each financial statement line item, and the effect on EPS

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the effect on net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosure is optional if the error is immaterial

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-50-7, when financial statements are restated for an error, the entity must disclose: (1) the nature of the error, (2) the effect of the correction on each financial statement line item and any per-share amounts affected, (3) the cumulative effect on retained earnings at the beginning of the earliest period presented, and (4) that previously issued statements have been restated.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-50-7に基づき、誤りの修正再表示時には以下を開示する：(1) 誤りの性質、(2) 各財務諸表項目およびEPSへの影響、(3) 表示する最も早い期間の期首利益剰余金への累積的影響、(4) 発行済財務諸表が修正再表示された旨。

questions.stem: A company retrospectively changes its inventory method. As a result of the change, profit-sharing bonuses (based on reported income) would differ from what was actually paid. Under ASC 250, how should these indirect effects be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Include in the retrospective adjustment to retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize in the period of the accounting change

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ignored entirely

choices.label: D
choices.text: Allocated to future periods

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-8, indirect effects of a change in accounting principle (such as changes to profit-sharing or royalty payments that depend on reported income) are recognized in the period the accounting change is made. They are NOT included in the retrospective application. Only the direct effects of the change (and related income tax effects) are applied retrospectively.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-8に基づき、会計方針変更の間接的影響（報告利益に連動する利益配分や使用料の変動等）は会計変更を行った期間に認識する。遡及適用には含めない。直接的影響（および関連する税効果）のみが遡及適用の対象。

questions.stem: Under SEC Staff Accounting Bulletin No. 99 (SAB 99), which of the following is correct regarding materiality of misstatements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A misstatement below 5% of net income is always immaterial

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both quantitative and qualitative factors must be considered

choices.label: C
choices.text: Materiality is determined solely by the dollar amount

choices.label: D
choices.text: Intentional misstatements are immaterial if below a threshold

questions.explanation: SAB 99 clarifies that materiality cannot be determined by a single quantitative threshold (such as 5% of income). Both quantitative and qualitative factors must be considered, including: whether the misstatement masks a change in earnings trend, converts a loss into income, relates to a significant segment, involves concealment of an unlawful transaction, or affects compliance with loan covenants.
questions.explanationJa: SAB 99は、重要性は単一の定量的閾値（例：利益の5%）だけでは判定できないことを明確にした。定量的要因と定性的要因の両方を考慮する必要がある：利益トレンドの変化を隠すか、損失を利益に変えるか、重要なセグメントに関するか、違法取引の隠蔽に関わるか、借入契約条件への影響があるか等。

questions.subtopic: Change in Useful Life After Impairment
questions.stem: Equipment with original cost of $500,000 and accumulated depreciation of $300,000 is impaired. Fair value is determined to be $120,000. After recording the impairment, the remaining useful life is revised from 5 to 3 years. What is the annual depreciation after the impairment?
questions.explanation: After impairment, the new carrying value becomes $120,000 (fair value). Impairment loss = ($500,000 − $300,000) − $120,000 = $80,000. The revised remaining useful life is 3 years. Annual depreciation = $120,000 / 3 = $40,000. Both the impairment (write-down to fair value) and the revised useful life (change in estimate) are applied going forward.
questions.explanationJa: 減損後の新帳簿価額は$120,000（公正価値）。減損損失 = ($500,000 − $300,000) − $120,000 = $80,000。改訂残存耐用年数 = 3年。年間減価償却 = $120,000 / 3 = $40,000。減損（公正価値への評価減）と耐用年数の改訂（見積りの変更）の両方を将来に向かって適用する。

questions.subtopic: Cumulative Effect - Adoption of New Standard
questions.stem: A company adopts a new FASB standard that requires modified retrospective transition with a cumulative-effect adjustment. The pre-tax cumulative effect at the adoption date is a $150,000 decrease to revenue. The tax rate is 25%. What is the adjustment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease beginning retained earnings by $150,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease beginning retained earnings by $112,500

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease current year revenue by $150,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease beginning retained earnings by $37,500

questions.explanation: Under modified retrospective transition, the cumulative effect is recorded as an adjustment to beginning retained earnings at the adoption date, with no restatement of prior periods. The after-tax adjustment = $150,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $112,500 decrease to beginning retained earnings. The $37,500 tax effect reduces the deferred tax liability (or increases DTA).
questions.explanationJa: 修正遡及的移行法では、累積的影響を適用日の期首利益剰余金への調整として記録し、過年度の修正再表示は行わない。税引後調整 = $150,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $112,500の期首利益剰余金減少。$37,500の税効果は繰延税金負債を減少（またはDTAを増加）させる。

questions.stem: When an entity voluntarily changes from one generally accepted accounting principle to another, how is the change generally accounted for under ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively, by adjusting prior period financial statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively, from the date of change forward

choices.label: C
choices.text: By recording a cumulative effect in current period income

choices.label: D
choices.text: By disclosing the change in the notes only with no adjustment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-5, a voluntary change in accounting principle is generally accounted for retrospectively by adjusting all prior periods presented as if the new principle had always been used. The cumulative effect on periods prior to those presented is reflected as an adjustment to beginning retained earnings of the earliest period presented. Prospective treatment is used only when retrospective application is impracticable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250に基づき、会計方針の自発的変更は原則として遡及適用され、表示されるすべての過年度財務諸表を新方針に基づき修正再表示する。表示期間以前の累積影響は最も早い期間の期首利益剰余金で調整する。

questions.stem: Bell Corp. originally estimated a machine's useful life at 10 years with no salvage value. After 4 years, Bell revises the remaining useful life to 4 years (8 years total). The original cost was $200,000. What is the annual depreciation expense after the revision?
questions.explanation: A change in estimated useful life is a change in accounting estimate, accounted for prospectively (ASC 250-10-45-17). After 4 years, accumulated depreciation = $200,000 ÷ 10 × 4 = $80,000. Remaining book value = $200,000 − $80,000 = $120,000. New annual depreciation = $120,000 ÷ 4 remaining years = $30,000. No prior period adjustment is made.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数の変更は会計上の見積りの変更であり、将来に向けて処理する。4年経過後の帳簿価額=$200,000−$80,000=$120,000。新年間減価償却費=$120,000÷残存4年=$30,000。過年度の修正はしない。

questions.stem: During Year 3, Carr Corp. discovers that $50,000 of expenses incurred in Year 1 were not recorded until Year 2. How should Carr correct this error in Year 3 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate the Year 1 and Year 2 comparative financial statements if presented

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the correction in Year 3 income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Adjust only Year 2 comparative statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the error in notes without restating

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-23, correction of an error in previously issued financial statements is made by restating the prior period financial statements. Both Year 1 (the year the expense should have been recorded) and Year 2 (the year it was incorrectly recorded) should be restated when presented as comparatives. This is different from a change in estimate, which is prospective.
questions.explanationJa: 過年度財務諸表の誤謬訂正は、過年度の修正再表示により行う。比較情報として表示する場合、Year 1（費用を計上すべきだった年）とYear 2（誤って計上された年）の両方を修正再表示する。

questions.stem: An entity changes its depreciation method from double-declining balance to straight-line. Under ASC 250, how is this change classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle—accounted for prospectively

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change in accounting principle—accounted for retrospectively

choices.label: C
choices.text: An error correction—accounted for by restating prior periods

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in reporting entity—accounted for retrospectively

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-18 specifies that a change in depreciation method is a change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle. Because the change reflects a revision in the estimated pattern of consumption of future benefits, it is accounted for prospectively—the same treatment as a change in estimate. This is one of the key exceptions to the general retrospective treatment of principle changes.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却方法の変更は「会計方針の変更を通じた会計上の見積りの変更」に分類され、将来に向けて処理する。将来の経済的便益の消費パターンに関する見積りの修正を反映するため、見積り変更と同じ扱いとなる。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, when is an entity permitted to apply a change in accounting principle prospectively rather than retrospectively?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When retrospective application is impracticable despite the entity's reasonable efforts

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the change is immaterial to any individual prior period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Whenever the entity's management prefers prospective application

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the change relates to a newly issued accounting standard

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-9 provides the impracticability exception: if retrospective application requires assumptions about management's intent in a prior period that cannot be objectively verified, or if it requires significant estimates for which the necessary information cannot be developed, retrospective application is impracticable. In such cases, the entity applies the change prospectively from the earliest date practicable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-9の不適用例外：遡及適用に過年度の経営者の意図に関する客観的に検証不能な仮定が必要な場合、または必要な情報を作成できない重要な見積りが必要な場合、遡及適用は実務上不可能とされ、将来適用が認められる。

questions.stem: Which of the following is an example of a change in reporting entity under ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Presenting consolidated financial statements in place of individual company statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Changing from FIFO to LIFO for inventory

choices.label: C
choices.text: Correcting an error in a subsidiary's depreciation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Revising the estimated useful life of an asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-21, a change in reporting entity includes: presenting consolidated statements instead of individual company statements, changing the specific subsidiaries included in consolidated statements, or combining entities that were not previously combined. Such changes are reported by restating all prior periods presented to show information for the new reporting entity.
questions.explanationJa: 報告単位の変更には、個別財務諸表から連結財務諸表への変更、連結対象子会社の変更等が含まれる。すべての表示過年度を新報告単位の情報で修正再表示して報告する。

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, ending inventory was overstated by $40,000. Assuming the error was not corrected and the Year 2 ending inventory is correct, what is the effect on Year 2 net income (ignoring taxes)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Understated by $40,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $40,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect because the error self-corrects

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $80,000

questions.explanation: Year 1 ending inventory = Year 2 beginning inventory. If Year 1 ending inventory is overstated by $40,000, Year 2 beginning inventory is also overstated by $40,000. Overstated beginning inventory leads to overstated COGS in Year 2, which understates Year 2 net income by $40,000. While the error self-corrects over the two-year period (Year 1 NI overstated + Year 2 NI understated = net zero), each individual year is misstated.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1の期末棚卸資産の$40,000過大計上→Year 2の期首棚卸資産も$40,000過大→Year 2のCOGSが$40,000過大→Year 2の純利益は$40,000過小。2年間では自己修正されるが、各年度は個別に誤っている。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, what must an entity demonstrate to justify a voluntary change in accounting principle?

choices.label: A
choices.text: That the new principle is preferable based on the circumstances

choices.label: B
choices.text: That the old principle was applied incorrectly

choices.label: C
choices.text: That the new principle results in lower tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: That industry peers use the new principle

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-2 requires that a voluntary change in accounting principle be made only if the entity can justify that the newly adopted principle is preferable in the circumstances. The presumption is that an accounting principle once adopted should not be changed unless the entity demonstrates that an alternative principle is clearly preferable. Lower taxes alone or peer practices alone are not sufficient justification.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-2により、会計方針の自発的変更は、新方針が状況に照らしてより望ましいことを正当化できる場合にのみ認められる。一度採用した方針は、代替方針が明確に望ましいと示さない限り変更すべきでないという推定がある。

questions.stem: Knox Corp. discovers in Year 3 that equipment purchased in Year 1 for $300,000 was incorrectly expensed instead of capitalized. The equipment has a 5-year useful life, no salvage value, and straight-line depreciation. Ignoring taxes, what is the adjustment to Year 3 beginning retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase of $180,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $300,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $240,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase of $120,000

questions.explanation: The equipment should have been capitalized at $300,000 in Year 1 and depreciated $60,000/year. By the beginning of Year 3 (end of Year 2): Cumulative depreciation = 2 years × $60,000 = $120,000. Net book value = $300,000 − $120,000 = $180,000. The original expensing reduced income by $300,000 in Year 1, but correct treatment would have reduced income by only $120,000 over Years 1-2. Net effect: retained earnings is understated by $300,000 − $120,000 = $180,000.
questions.explanationJa: 設備は$300,000で資本化し年$60,000償却すべきだった。Year 3期首時点：累計減価償却=$120,000、帳簿価額=$180,000。Year 1で全額費用処理したことで利益剰余金が$180,000過小計上されている（$300,000費用−$120,000正規償却）。

questions.stem: When an entity makes a change in accounting principle, which of the following disclosures is required by ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The nature and reason for the change, the method of applying the change, and the effect on each financial statement line item affected

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the nature of the change and the cumulative effect on retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only a general statement that the accounting principle was changed

choices.label: D
choices.text: The change amount and the names of auditors who approved the change

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-50-1 requires extensive disclosures including: the nature of and reason for the change, the method of applying the change (retrospective or prospective), a description of the prior period information that has been retrospectively adjusted, and the effect of the change on income from continuing operations, net income, and any other affected financial statement line item, including per-share amounts.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-50-1は、変更の性質と理由、適用方法、遡及修正された過年度情報の説明、継続事業利益・純利益・その他影響を受ける財務諸表項目（1株当たり金額を含む）への影響の開示を要求する。

questions.stem: How should a change in the estimated bad debt percentage be distinguished from a correction of an error in recording bad debts?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in estimate reflects new information or developments; an error reflects a mistake in applying the existing methodology

choices.label: B
choices.text: There is no distinction; both are treated the same way

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in estimate is always immaterial; an error is always material

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in estimate requires retrospective treatment; an error requires prospective treatment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250, a change in accounting estimate (prospective treatment) results from new information, changed circumstances, or more experience. An error correction (retrospective restatement) involves a mathematical mistake, a mistake in applying GAAP, or an oversight of available facts. If management revises its bad debt estimate based on updated collection experience, it's a change in estimate. If the original estimate was based on a calculation error, it's an error.
questions.explanationJa: 見積りの変更は新情報や状況の変化に基づくもの（将来処理）、誤謬は計算ミスやGAAP適用の誤り（修正再表示）。貸倒率の変更が回収実績の更新に基づけば見積り変更、計算ミスに基づけば誤謬訂正となる。

questions.stem: During the third quarter, Webb Inc. discovers a material error that originated in the first quarter of the same fiscal year. How should Webb correct this error in its third quarter interim financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate the first quarter financial data if presented in comparative interim statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the entire correction as a third quarter adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Spread the correction equally over the remaining two quarters

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer the correction to the annual financial statements

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-25 and ASC 270-10-45-16 require that when an error is discovered in an interim period that affects a previously reported interim period of the current fiscal year, the affected interim period's financial data should be restated when presented in comparative form. The error should be allocated to the period in which it occurred, not lumped into the quarter of discovery.
questions.explanationJa: 当事業年度内の前四半期に係る誤謬が発見された場合、比較中間財務諸表として表示する際に当該四半期のデータを修正再表示する。誤謬は発生した期間に配分し、発見した四半期に一括計上しない。

questions.stem: Roth Corp. changes its revenue recognition method, which also triggers a change in the royalty payments (calculated as a percentage of revenue) owed to a licensor. How should Roth account for the change in royalty payments?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize the indirect effect in the period of the accounting change, not in the restated prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Include the royalty changes in the retrospective restatement of prior periods

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ignore the indirect effect entirely

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the indirect effect as an extraordinary item

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-8, indirect effects of a change in accounting principle (such as changes in royalty payments, profit-sharing, or bonuses that are contractually based on reported amounts) are recognized in the period of the change. They are NOT included in the retrospective application to prior periods. Only the direct effects of the change (and related income tax effects) are applied retrospectively.
questions.explanationJa: 会計方針変更の間接的影響（報告金額に基づく契約上のロイヤルティ・利益分配等の変更）は変更期間に認識し、過年度の遡及修正には含めない。遡及適用されるのは変更の直接的影響（および関連する法人税効果）のみ。

questions.stem: When a new ASU requires adoption and specifies that transition is on a modified retrospective basis, what does this typically mean?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record a cumulative catch-up adjustment to beginning retained earnings at the adoption date without restating prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate all prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: Apply the new standard only to transactions occurring after the adoption date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Choose between full retrospective and prospective application

questions.explanation: Modified retrospective transition is a common approach specified by new ASUs (e.g., ASC 606, ASC 842). Under this approach, the entity recognizes the cumulative effect of initially applying the new standard as an adjustment to beginning retained earnings (or net assets) at the date of adoption. Prior period financial statements are not restated but enhanced disclosures comparing prior-method to new-method results are typically required.
questions.explanationJa: 修正遡及的移行法では、新基準適用の累積影響を適用日の期首利益剰余金への調整として認識し、過年度の修正再表示は行わない。ASC 606やASC 842など多くの新基準で採用されている移行方法。

questions.stem: Which of the following errors is a non-counterbalancing error that will NOT self-correct over two periods?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Failure to record depreciation on equipment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstatement of ending inventory

choices.label: C
choices.text: Failure to record accrued wages at year-end

choices.label: D
choices.text: Failure to record prepaid insurance at year-end

questions.explanation: Failure to record depreciation is a non-counterbalancing error because the understatement of depreciation expense accumulates each year—it does not reverse in the following period. The asset remains overstated until corrected or disposed of. In contrast, inventory errors, accrued expenses, and prepaid expenses are counterbalancing errors that self-correct over two consecutive periods because the ending balance of one period becomes the beginning balance of the next.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却の未計上は非相殺的誤謬であり、2期間では自己修正されない。減価償却費の過小計上は毎年累積し、資産は訂正または処分まで過大計上のままとなる。棚卸資産・未払費用・前払費用の誤謬は2期間で自己修正される相殺的誤謬である。

questions.subtopic: Change in Accounting Principle - Tax Effect
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Granger Corp. voluntarily changed its inventory method from LIFO to FIFO. The cumulative pre-tax effect as of January 1, Year 3, was a $200,000 increase in inventory. The company's tax rate is 25%. What is the net-of-tax adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings in Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $200,000 increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: $150,000 increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: $50,000 increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: $150,000 decrease

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-5, a voluntary change in accounting principle is applied retrospectively. The cumulative effect is recorded as an adjustment to opening retained earnings. The pre-tax cumulative effect is $200,000 (inventory increase). The tax effect is $200,000 x 25% = $50,000 (deferred tax liability increase). The net-of-tax adjustment to retained earnings = $200,000 - $50,000 = $150,000 increase. The entry is: Dr Inventory $200,000 / Cr Deferred Tax Liability $50,000 / Cr Retained Earnings $150,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-5に基づき、任意の会計方針変更は遡及適用する。累積影響額は期首利益剰余金への調整として計上する。税引前の累積影響額は$200,000（棚卸資産の増加）。税効果は$200,000 × 25% = $50,000（繰延税金負債の増加）。利益剰余金への税引後調整額 = $200,000 - $50,000 = $150,000の増加。仕訳：Dr 棚卸資産 $200,000 / Cr 繰延税金負債 $50,000 / Cr 利益剰余金 $150,000。

questions.stem: At the end of Year 3, Belmont Inc. discovered that depreciation expense on a building had been overstated by $30,000 per year for Years 1 and 2. The building was purchased at the beginning of Year 1. The tax rate is 30%. What is the net-of-tax prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings in Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $42,000 increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: $60,000 increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: $18,000 increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: $42,000 decrease

questions.explanation: Depreciation was overstated by $30,000 per year for 2 years, totaling $60,000. This means the accumulated depreciation was overstated by $60,000 and net income was understated by $60,000 over the two years. The tax effect: $60,000 x 30% = $18,000 (taxes were underpaid, creating a deferred tax liability). Net-of-tax adjustment to retained earnings = $60,000 - $18,000 = $42,000 increase. The correcting entry includes: Dr Accumulated Depreciation $60,000 / Cr Deferred Tax Liability $18,000 / Cr Retained Earnings $42,000.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却費がYear 1とYear 2で各$30,000過大計上され、合計$60,000の過大計上。これにより減価償却累計額が$60,000過大、純利益が$60,000過少となっていた。税効果：$60,000 × 30% = $18,000（税金過少支払い、繰延税金負債が発生）。利益剰余金への税引後調整額 = $60,000 - $18,000 = $42,000の増加。訂正仕訳：Dr 減価償却累計額 $60,000 / Cr 繰延税金負債 $18,000 / Cr 利益剰余金 $42,000。

questions.stem: During Year 2, Parkway Corp. discovered that it failed to accrue $75,000 of salaries payable at December 31, Year 1. The salaries were paid in January Year 2 and were expensed in Year 2 when paid. What is the effect of this error on Year 2 net income before any correction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income is overstated by $75,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income is understated by $75,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net income is correctly stated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net income is overstated by $150,000

questions.explanation: At December 31, Year 1, the $75,000 accrued salary was not recorded, so Year 1 expenses were understated by $75,000 (Year 1 income overstated by $75,000). When the salaries were paid in January Year 2, the full $75,000 was expensed in Year 2. However, the correct Year 2 treatment should have zero salary expense for this amount (since it should have been a Year 1 expense reducing the payable). Therefore, Year 2 expenses are overstated by $75,000, and Year 2 net income is understated by $75,000. This is a counterbalancing error.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1末に$75,000の未払給与が未計上のため、Year 1の費用は$75,000過少（Year 1の純利益は$75,000過大）。Year 2の1月に支払時に全額$75,000をYear 2の費用として計上。しかし正しくはYear 1の費用であるため、Year 2の費用が$75,000過大となり、Year 2の純利益は$75,000過少となる。これは相殺的誤謬であり、2期間で自己修正される。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Residual Value Revision
questions.stem: Jensen Co. purchased equipment on January 1, Year 1, for $490,000 with an estimated useful life of 10 years and no residual value. Straight-line depreciation is used. On January 1, Year 4, the residual value estimate is revised to $49,000. What is the depreciation expense for Year 4?
questions.explanation: Original annual depreciation = ($490,000 - $0) / 10 = $49,000 per year. Accumulated depreciation through Year 3 = $49,000 x 3 = $147,000. Book value at January 1, Year 4 = $490,000 - $147,000 = $343,000. Under ASC 250-10-45-17, a change in residual value is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively. Revised residual value = $49,000. Remaining useful life = 10 - 3 = 7 years. New annual depreciation = ($343,000 - $49,000) / 7 = $294,000 / 7 = $42,000. Prior periods are not restated for changes in estimates.

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, when is it considered impracticable to apply a change in accounting principle retrospectively?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When management determines the cost of retroactive application exceeds the benefits

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the entity cannot determine the cumulative effect of applying the change to prior periods after making every reasonable effort

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the change involves a new FASB standard with a specified effective date

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the entity has been in operation for fewer than three years

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-9, retrospective application is considered impracticable when the entity cannot determine the effects on prior periods after making every reasonable effort to do so. This occurs when (a) the effects cannot be determined, (b) retrospective application requires assumptions about management's intent in a prior period that cannot be independently substantiated, or (c) retrospective application requires significant estimates for a prior period and the necessary information cannot be developed objectively. A cost-benefit analysis is not the basis for the impracticability exception.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-9に基づき、遡及適用が実行不可能（impracticable）とみなされるのは、あらゆる合理的な努力を行っても過年度への影響額を算定できない場合である。(a) 影響額を算定できない、(b) 過年度における経営者の意図に関する仮定が必要で独立して実証できない、(c) 重要な見積りが必要だが客観的に情報を入手できない場合が該当する。コスト・ベネフィット分析は実行不可能の根拠とはならない。

questions.stem: When a public company makes a voluntary change in accounting principle, what role does the independent auditor play regarding the preferability of the new principle?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The auditor must approve the change before it can be adopted

choices.label: B
choices.text: The auditor provides a preferability letter indicating the new method is preferable under the circumstances

choices.label: C
choices.text: The auditor has no responsibility regarding the preferability assessment

choices.label: D
choices.text: The auditor must recalculate all affected prior-period financial statements

questions.explanation: Under SEC requirements (Rule 10-01(b)(6) of Regulation S-X), when a registrant makes a voluntary change in accounting principle, the independent auditor must provide a preferability letter indicating that the newly adopted accounting principle is preferable in the circumstances. This letter is filed with the first Form 10-Q after the change. The auditor does not approve the change but rather evaluates and acknowledges that the new principle is preferable to the previously used principle.
questions.explanationJa: SEC規則（Regulation S-Xの規則10-01(b)(6)）に基づき、公開会社が任意に会計方針を変更する場合、独立監査人は新方針がその状況下でより望ましい（preferable）ことを示す望ましさの確認書（preferability letter）を提出する。この書簡は変更後最初のForm 10-Qに添付される。監査人は変更を承認するのではなく、新方針が従前の方針より望ましいことを評価・確認する役割を果たす。

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 2, Newton Co. discovered that a $120,000 accounts receivable that should have been written off as uncollectible in Year 1 was still carried on the books. The allowance for doubtful accounts had a balance of $200,000 at December 31, Year 1 (before correction). What is the prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings in Year 2 (ignoring taxes)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $120,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $120,000 increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: $0 — no effect on retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: $80,000 decrease

questions.explanation: Writing off a specific account receivable against an existing allowance for doubtful accounts has no effect on net income or retained earnings. The write-off entry is: Dr Allowance for Doubtful Accounts $120,000 / Cr Accounts Receivable $120,000. This entry reduces both the gross receivable and the allowance by the same amount, leaving net realizable value and net income unchanged. Since the allowance balance of $200,000 was sufficient to absorb the $120,000 write-off, no additional bad debt expense is needed. Therefore, retained earnings is not affected.
questions.explanationJa: 既存の貸倒引当金に対して特定の売掛金を貸倒処理しても、純利益や利益剰余金に影響はない。仕訳：Dr 貸倒引当金 $120,000 / Cr 売掛金 $120,000。この仕訳は売掛金総額と引当金を同額減少させるため、正味実現可能価額と純利益は不変。引当金残高$200,000は$120,000の貸倒処理を十分に吸収できるため、追加の貸倒費用は不要。したがって利益剰余金に影響はない。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Carter Inc. issued $1,000,000 of 5-year bonds at a premium of $80,000. The company incorrectly expensed the entire premium to interest expense in Year 1 instead of amortizing it over 5 years using the straight-line method. At December 31, Year 2, what is the pre-tax correction to retained earnings for this error?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $48,000 increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: $64,000 increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: $32,000 increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: $80,000 increase

questions.explanation: The bond premium of $80,000 should be amortized over 5 years at $16,000 per year using straight-line. In Year 1, the company incorrectly expensed the entire $80,000 premium, instead of amortizing only $16,000. This means Year 1 interest expense was overstated by $64,000 ($80,000 - $16,000). At December 31, Year 2, the cumulative error on retained earnings must account for Year 1 and Year 2. Year 1 error: $64,000 overstatement of expense. Year 2: Since the premium was fully written off in Year 1, no premium amortization was recorded in Year 2, but $16,000 should have been recorded. So Year 2 expense was understated by $16,000. Net effect on retained earnings through Year 2 = $64,000 - $16,000 = $48,000 understatement of retained earnings. The correction is a $48,000 increase to retained earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 社債プレミアム$80,000は5年間にわたり定額法で年$16,000ずつ償却すべき。Year 1に会社はプレミアム全額$80,000を費用処理したため、$16,000のみ償却すべきところ$64,000($80,000-$16,000)の費用過大計上。Year 2ではプレミアムがYear 1で全額償却済みのため、本来計上すべき$16,000の償却が未計上で費用が$16,000過少。Year 2末時点の利益剰余金への累積影響 = $64,000 - $16,000 = $48,000の過少計上。訂正額は利益剰余金$48,000の増加。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, when a parent company spins off a subsidiary, resulting in a change in reporting entity, how should comparative financial statements be presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prior periods are not restated; the change is applied prospectively only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Financial statements of all prior periods presented are restated to reflect the new reporting entity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the immediately preceding period is restated

choices.label: D
choices.text: A cumulative effect adjustment is recorded in current-period income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-21, a change in reporting entity requires retrospective application. Financial statements of all prior periods presented must be restated to show the financial information for the new reporting entity for all periods. This ensures comparability across periods. A spin-off that results in a change in the composition of the reporting entity triggers this requirement, and the nature of and reason for the change must be disclosed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-21に基づき、報告企業の変更は遡及適用が必要。表示されるすべての過年度の財務諸表を、新しい報告企業の情報を反映するよう修正再表示する。これにより期間間の比較可能性が確保される。報告企業の構成変更をもたらすスピンオフはこの要件の対象となり、変更の内容と理由を開示する必要がある。

questions.subtopic: Change in Entity - Variable Interest Entity
questions.stem: Blake Corp. determines in Year 3 that it is the primary beneficiary of a variable interest entity (VIE) that it was previously not required to consolidate. Under ASC 250, this change in reporting entity should be accounted for by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospective consolidation from Year 3 forward with no restatement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restating all prior-period financial statements to include the VIE

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recording a cumulative catch-up adjustment in Year 3 earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosing the change in the notes only, with no financial statement adjustment

questions.explanation: A change in the entities included in consolidated financial statements constitutes a change in reporting entity under ASC 250-10-45-21. Such changes require retrospective application — all prior-period financial statements presented must be restated to include the VIE as if it had been consolidated in those periods. This ensures users can compare financial data across periods on a consistent basis. The entity must also disclose the nature of and reason for the change in reporting entity.
questions.explanationJa: 連結財務諸表に含まれる企業の変更はASC 250-10-45-21に基づく報告企業の変更に該当する。遡及適用が必要であり、表示されるすべての過年度の財務諸表を、当該VIEがそれらの期間に連結されていたかのように修正再表示する。これにより利用者は一貫した基準で期間間の財務データを比較できる。報告企業の変更の内容と理由の開示も必要。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Fulton Inc. purchased equipment for $240,000 with a 6-year useful life and no residual value (straight-line depreciation). The company incorrectly expensed the entire $240,000 in Year 1. The error was discovered at the beginning of Year 3. Ignoring taxes, what is the correcting entry to retained earnings at January 1, Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $160,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $200,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $240,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit Retained Earnings $80,000

questions.explanation: The equipment should have been capitalized at $240,000 and depreciated over 6 years at $40,000 per year. Year 1 impact: The company expensed $240,000, but should have recorded only $40,000 depreciation. So Year 1 expenses were overstated by $200,000 (retained earnings understated by $200,000). Year 2 impact: No depreciation was recorded (asset was already written off), but $40,000 should have been recorded. So Year 2 expenses were understated by $40,000 (retained earnings overstated by $40,000). Net effect on retained earnings at January 1, Year 3 = $200,000 - $40,000 = $160,000 understatement. The correcting entry credits Retained Earnings $160,000, debits Equipment $240,000, and credits Accumulated Depreciation $80,000 ($40,000 x 2 years).
questions.explanationJa: 設備は$240,000で資産計上し、6年間にわたり年$40,000で減価償却すべきであった。Year 1の影響：会社は$240,000を費用処理したが、正しくは$40,000の減価償却のみ。費用が$200,000過大（利益剰余金$200,000過少）。Year 2の影響：減価償却未計上（資産は既に償却済み）だが、$40,000を計上すべき。費用が$40,000過少（利益剰余金$40,000過大）。Year 3期首の利益剰余金への累積影響 = $200,000 - $40,000 = $160,000の過少計上。訂正仕訳：Dr 設備 $240,000 / Cr 減価償却累計額 $80,000 / Cr 利益剰余金 $160,000。

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 1, Mason Corp. failed to record a $100,000 land purchase, instead expensing the amount. The error was discovered at the end of Year 4. Ignoring taxes, what is the cumulative effect of this non-counterbalancing error on retained earnings at December 31, Year 4, if no correction has been made?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retained earnings is understated by $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retained earnings is overstated by $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retained earnings is correctly stated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retained earnings is understated by $25,000 per year

questions.explanation: Land is a non-depreciable asset. When the $100,000 land purchase was expensed in Year 1, expenses were overstated by $100,000, causing Year 1 net income and retained earnings to be understated by $100,000. Because land is not depreciated, there is no offsetting effect in subsequent years. This is a classic non-counterbalancing error — it does not self-correct over time. At December 31, Year 4, retained earnings remains understated by $100,000 and the land asset is understated by $100,000 until the error is corrected.
questions.explanationJa: 土地は非償却資産。$100,000の土地購入をYear 1に費用処理したため、費用が$100,000過大となり、Year 1の純利益と利益剰余金が$100,000過少。土地は減価償却されないため、後年度に相殺効果はない。これは典型的な非相殺的誤謬であり、時間の経過で自己修正されない。Year 4末時点で利益剰余金は$100,000過少、土地資産も$100,000過少のまま、誤謬が訂正されるまで継続する。

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 2, Harper Inc. discovered that $180,000 of revenue recognized in Year 1 should have been deferred to Year 2 because performance obligations were not satisfied until Year 2. What is the effect of correcting this error on Year 2 reported net income (ignoring taxes)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Year 2 net income increases by $180,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Year 2 net income decreases by $180,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Year 2 net income is not affected

choices.label: D
choices.text: Year 2 net income increases by $360,000

questions.explanation: When correcting this error, the Year 1 overstatement of revenue is corrected through a prior period adjustment to opening retained earnings of Year 2 (debit Retained Earnings $180,000, credit Deferred Revenue $180,000 — at January 1, Year 2). Then, the revenue is properly recognized in Year 2 when the performance obligation is satisfied (debit Deferred Revenue $180,000, credit Revenue $180,000). However, the revenue would have already been reported in Year 2 under the error (since the cash or receivable was already collected and the company likely recognized it again). Under proper error correction, Year 2 reported net income on the restated financial statements reflects only the revenue that was properly earned in Year 2. The $180,000 is correctly reported as Year 2 revenue, so Year 2 net income as properly restated is not affected.
questions.explanationJa: この誤謬を訂正する際、Year 1の収益過大計上はYear 2の期首利益剰余金への修正（前期修正）として処理する（Dr 利益剰余金 $180,000 / Cr 繰延収益 $180,000 — Year 2期首時点）。そしてYear 2に履行義務が充足された時点で適切に収益認識する（Dr 繰延収益 $180,000 / Cr 収益 $180,000）。修正再表示後のYear 2の財務諸表では、$180,000はYear 2の収益として適切に報告されるため、Year 2の純利益に影響はない。

questions.stem: On December 31, Year 1, Porter Corp. received goods costing $90,000 that were included in the physical inventory count but the corresponding purchase was not recorded until January Year 2. What is the effect of this error on Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income overstated by $90,000 and liabilities understated by $90,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income understated by $90,000 and assets overstated by $90,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net income correctly stated and liabilities understated by $90,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net income overstated by $90,000 and assets overstated by $90,000

questions.explanation: The goods were included in ending inventory (correct), but the purchase (accounts payable) was not recorded. Since ending inventory is correct, cost of goods sold is not directly affected by the inventory count. However, the purchase was not recorded: the credit to Accounts Payable was missed, and the debit to Purchases (or Inventory under perpetual) was also missed. Under periodic inventory, COGS = Beginning Inventory + Purchases - Ending Inventory. Since purchases were understated by $90,000 but ending inventory was correctly counted, COGS is understated by $90,000, causing net income to be overstated by $90,000. Accounts payable (liabilities) is also understated by $90,000.
questions.explanationJa: 商品は期末棚卸に含まれている（正しい）が、仕入（買掛金）が未計上。定期棚卸法の場合、売上原価 = 期首棚卸 + 仕入 - 期末棚卸。仕入が$90,000過少だが期末棚卸は正しいため、売上原価が$90,000過少となり、純利益が$90,000過大。買掛金（負債）も$90,000過少。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Bad Debt Rate
questions.stem: Riley Corp. has historically estimated bad debt expense at 2% of credit sales. At the beginning of Year 4, based on recent collection experience, the company revises the estimate to 3% of credit sales. Year 4 credit sales are $2,000,000. Under ASC 250, how should Riley account for this change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate prior years using 3% and record a cumulative adjustment to retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the 3% rate prospectively; Year 4 bad debt expense is $60,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Apply the 3% rate prospectively; Year 4 bad debt expense is $20,000 (incremental only)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record an error correction for the difference in prior years' estimates

questions.explanation: A change in the estimated bad debt percentage is a change in accounting estimate under ASC 250-10-45-17. Changes in estimates are applied prospectively — the new rate is used from Year 4 forward. Prior periods are not restated because the original estimates were based on the best information available at the time. Year 4 bad debt expense = $2,000,000 x 3% = $60,000. The full 3% rate is applied to Year 4 sales, not just the 1% incremental difference.
questions.explanationJa: 貸倒見積率の変更はASC 250-10-45-17に基づく会計上の見積りの変更。見積りの変更は将来に向かって適用 — 新しい率をYear 4以降に使用する。過年度の見積りはその時点で入手可能な最善の情報に基づいていたため修正再表示しない。Year 4の貸倒費用 = $2,000,000 × 3% = $60,000。増分の1%のみではなく、3%の全率をYear 4の売上に適用する。

questions.stem: Webb Corp. changes from FIFO to average cost for inventory valuation on January 1, Year 3. The cumulative pre-tax effect at January 1, Year 3, is a $300,000 decrease in inventory. The tax rate is 25%. In the comparative Year 2 financial statements (being restated), the pre-tax inventory change attributable to Year 2 alone is a $120,000 decrease. What adjustment is made to Year 2 income tax expense in the restated Year 2 income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $30,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $75,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $120,000 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: $30,000 increase

questions.explanation: When restating Year 2 comparatives, only the Year 2 portion of the change is reflected in the Year 2 income statement. The pre-tax effect attributable to Year 2 is a $120,000 decrease in inventory, which means COGS increased by $120,000 and pre-tax income decreased by $120,000.
