The tax effect on Year 2 = $120,000 x 25% = $30,000. Since pre-tax income decreased, income tax expense also decreases by $30,000. The restated Year 2 income statement shows $30,000 less in income tax expense.
questions.explanationJa: Year 2の比較財務諸表を修正再表示する際、Year 2に帰属する部分のみをYear 2の損益計算書に反映する。Year 2に帰属する税引前影響額は棚卸資産$120,000の減少で、売上原価が$120,000増加し税引前利益が$120,000減少。Year 2の税効果 = $120,000 × 25% = $30,000。税引前利益が減少するため、法人税費用も$30,000減少する。修正再表示後のYear 2損益計算書は法人税費用が$30,000少なくなる。

questions.subtopic: Error Discovery - Interim vs Annual Period
questions.stem: During the third quarter of Year 2, Evans Corp. discovers an error that affected the first quarter of Year 2. The error overstated first-quarter revenue by $50,000. How should this be corrected in the Year 2 interim financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record the entire $50,000 correction in the third quarter

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the previously issued first-quarter interim financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: Defer the correction to the annual financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Allocate the correction evenly across the remaining quarters of Year 2

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-25 and ASC 270-10-45-16, when an error is discovered in a current-year interim period that relates to a previously reported interim period of the same fiscal year, the entity must restate the previously reported interim financial information. The first-quarter financial statements should be restated to correct the $50,000 revenue overstatement. The correction should not be recorded entirely in the quarter of discovery, allocated across remaining quarters, or deferred to year-end.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-25およびASC 270-10-45-16に基づき、同一会計年度内の過去に報告された中間期間に関する誤謬が発見された場合、過去に報告された中間期間の財務情報を修正再表示する。第1四半期の財務諸表を修正再表示し、$50,000の収益過大計上を訂正する。発見した四半期に全額計上する、残りの四半期に配分する、年次決算まで繰り延べるのいずれも適切ではない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Principle - Completed Contract to POC
questions.stem: A construction company changes from the completed-contract method to the percentage-of-completion method for recognizing revenue on long-term contracts. Under ASC 250, this change should be reported by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospective application to contracts entered into after the change date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospective application with adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings for the earliest period presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative effect adjustment to current-period income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Restatement of only the most recent prior period presented

questions.explanation: A change from completed-contract to percentage-of-completion is a change in accounting principle under ASC 250. Under ASC 250-10-45-5, voluntary changes in accounting principle are accounted for by retrospective application. The cumulative effect of the change is reflected as an adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings for the earliest period presented, and all prior periods presented in comparative financial statements are restated. This provides comparability across periods.
questions.explanationJa: 完成工事基準から工事進行基準への変更はASC 250に基づく会計方針の変更。ASC 250-10-45-5に基づき、任意の会計方針変更は遡及適用により処理する。変更の累積影響額は表示される最も古い期間の期首利益剰余金への調整として反映し、比較財務諸表に表示されるすべての過年度を修正再表示する。これにより期間間の比較可能性が確保される。

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 3, Doyle Corp. discovered that a lease commencing January 1, Year 1, was incorrectly classified as an operating lease when it should have been classified as a finance lease. The lease has annual payments of $50,000, a 5-year term, and the present value of lease payments at inception was $210,000. Straight-line amortization and a 6% interest rate apply. In Year 1, the company recorded $50,000 of rent expense. The correct Year 1 amounts are: amortization of right-of-use asset $42,000 and interest expense $12,600. What is the pre-tax effect of this error on Year 1 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income was overstated by $4,600

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income was understated by $4,600

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net income was overstated by $54,600

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net income was understated by $42,000

questions.explanation: Under the incorrect operating lease treatment, Year 1 total expense was $50,000 (straight-line rent expense). Under the correct finance lease treatment, Year 1 total expense = amortization of ROU asset $42,000 + interest expense $12,600 = $54,600. The company recorded $50,000 of expense but should have recorded $54,600. Expenses were understated by $4,600 ($54,600 - $50,000), which means Year 1 net income was overstated by $4,600. The correcting entry would increase total expense by $4,600 and decrease net income by $4,600.
questions.explanationJa: 誤ったオペレーティング・リース処理では、Year 1の費用合計は$50,000（定額賃借料）。正しいファイナンス・リース処理では、Year 1の費用合計 = 使用権資産の償却$42,000 + 利息費用$12,600 = $54,600。会社は$50,000の費用を計上したが、正しくは$54,600を計上すべきであった。費用が$4,600過少（$54,600 - $50,000）であり、Year 1の純利益は$4,600過大計上されていた。

questions.stem: Which of the following pension-related errors would require a prior period adjustment upon discovery?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in the discount rate assumption for measuring pension obligations

choices.label: B
choices.text: A mathematical error in calculating the projected benefit obligation in a prior year

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in the expected long-term rate of return on plan assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: An actuarial gain resulting from updated mortality tables

questions.explanation: A mathematical error in calculating the projected benefit obligation (PBO) is an error under ASC 250, requiring correction through a prior period adjustment with restatement. The other options are changes in estimates: changing the discount rate (A), changing the expected return assumption (C), and actuarial gains from updated assumptions (D) are all changes in accounting estimates that are accounted for prospectively. Only errors — mistakes in applying existing methods or mathematical mistakes — qualify for prior period adjustment treatment under ASC 250-10-45-23.
questions.explanationJa: 予測給付債務（PBO）の計算における数学的誤りはASC 250に基づく誤謬であり、前期修正による訂正（修正再表示）が必要。他の選択肢は見積りの変更：割引率の変更(A)、期待収益率の変更(C)、死亡率表更新による保険数理差益(D)はすべて会計上の見積りの変更であり、将来に向かって処理する。既存の方法の適用ミスや計算ミスのみがASC 250-10-45-23に基づく前期修正の対象となる。

questions.stem: Porter Inc. purchased machinery on January 1, Year 1, for $400,000 with a 5-year useful life and no residual value. The company used double-declining balance (DDB) depreciation. On January 1, Year 3, Porter changes to straight-line depreciation. What is the Year 3 straight-line depreciation expense?
questions.explanation: Under DDB, the rate is 2/5 = 40%. Year 1 depreciation = $400,000 x 40% = $160,000. Book value at end of Year 1 = $240,000. Year 2 depreciation = $240,000 x 40% = $96,000. Book value at end of Year 2 = $144,000. On January 1, Year 3, the change to straight-line is applied prospectively (change in estimate effected by a change in principle). Remaining book value = $144,000, residual value = $0, remaining life = 3 years. Year 3 straight-line depreciation = ($144,000 - $0) / 3 = $48,000.
questions.explanationJa: DDB法の償却率 = 2/5 = 40%。Year 1の減価償却 = $400,000 × 40% = $160,000。Year 1末の帳簿価額 = $240,000。Year 2の減価償却 = $240,000 × 40% = $96,000。Year 2末の帳簿価額 = $144,000。Year 3期首に定額法へ変更、将来に向かって適用（方針変更を伴う見積りの変更）。残存帳簿価額 = $144,000、残存価額 = $0、残存耐用年数 = 3年。Year 3の定額法減価償却 = ($144,000 - $0) / 3 = $48,000。

questions.subtopic: Big R Restatement vs Little r Revision
questions.stem: Under SEC guidance (SAB Topics 1.N and 1.M), what distinguishes a 'Big R' restatement from a 'little r' revision of previously issued financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A Big R restatement applies only to changes in accounting principles, while a little r revision applies to error corrections

choices.label: B
choices.text: A Big R restatement requires an Item 4.02 Form 8-K filing and re-issuance of financial statements, while a little r revision corrects immaterial errors in the next filing

choices.label: C
choices.text: A Big R restatement is prospective, while a little r revision is retrospective

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no distinction; the terms are interchangeable under SEC guidance

questions.explanation: Under SEC guidance, a 'Big R' restatement (SAB Topic 1.N) is required when prior-period financial statements contain material errors. It requires the company to file an Item 4.02 Form 8-K (non-reliance on previously issued financial statements) and reissue corrected financial statements. A 'little r' revision (SAB Topic 1.M) is used when errors are immaterial to the prior period but correcting them in the current period would be material. The entity revises the prior-period comparative financial statements in the next filing without a Form 8-K. The distinction hinges on whether the error is material to the originally issued financial statements.
questions.explanationJa: SEC指針に基づき、「Big R」修正再表示（SAB Topic 1.N）は過年度財務諸表に重要な誤謬がある場合に必要。Item 4.02 Form 8-K（過去に発行された財務諸表への非依存）の提出と訂正済み財務諸表の再発行が求められる。「little r」修正（SAB Topic 1.M）は、誤謬が過年度では重要でないが当期に訂正すると重要になる場合に使用。次回の提出で過年度の比較財務諸表を修正するがForm 8-Kは不要。区別は誤謬が当初発行された財務諸表にとって重要かどうかによる。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Revised Salvage Value
questions.stem: Quincy Co. purchased a delivery truck on January 1, Year 1, for $90,000 with an estimated 8-year life and $10,000 residual value, using straight-line depreciation. On January 1, Year 5, the company revised the residual value to $4,000 with no change to the total useful life. What is the depreciation expense for Year 5?
questions.explanation: Original annual depreciation = ($90,000 − $10,000) / 8 = $10,000. Accumulated depreciation through Year 4 = $10,000 × 4 = $40,000. Carrying amount on January 1, Year 5 = $90,000 − $40,000 = $50,000. A change in residual value is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively. Remaining life = 8 − 4 = 4 years; revised residual = $4,000. Year 5 depreciation = ($50,000 − $4,000) / 4 = $46,000 / 4 = $11,500.
questions.explanationJa: 当初の年間減価償却費 =（$90,000 − $10,000）/ 8 = $10,000。Year 4末までの減価償却累計額 = $10,000 × 4 = $40,000。Year 5期首の帳簿価額 = $90,000 − $40,000 = $50,000。残存価額の変更は将来に向かって適用する見積りの変更。残存年数 = 8 − 4 = 4年、改訂残存価額 = $4,000。Year 5の減価償却費 =（$50,000 − $4,000）/ 4 = $11,500。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate vs Change in Principle Classification
questions.stem: Which of the following is accounted for as a change in accounting estimate rather than a change in accounting principle under ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Switching inventory costing from FIFO to weighted average

choices.label: B
choices.text: Adopting the percentage-of-completion method in place of the completed-contract method

choices.label: C
choices.text: Revising the estimated period over which a deferred subscription cost is amortized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Changing from the cost method to the equity method when significant influence is first obtained

questions.explanation: Revising the amortization period for a deferred cost is a change in accounting estimate because it reflects new information about the asset's expected benefit period; it is applied prospectively under ASC 250-10-45-17. Switching inventory costing methods and adopting percentage-of-completion are changes in accounting principle requiring retrospective application. Moving from the cost method to the equity method when significant influence is first obtained is governed by ASC 323 and is applied prospectively as of the date influence is obtained, not treated as a change in estimate.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延費用の償却期間の改訂は、資産の予想便益期間に関する新たな情報を反映するため会計上の見積りの変更であり、ASC 250-10-45-17に基づき将来に向かって適用する。棚卸資産の原価計算方法の変更と工事進行基準の採用は遡及適用を要する会計方針の変更。重要な影響力を初めて獲得した際の原価法から持分法への移行はASC 323により影響力獲得日時点から将来に向かって適用され、見積りの変更とはしない。

questions.stem: Sutton Corp. paid $36,000 on October 1, Year 1, for a one-year service contract and expensed the entire amount immediately. The contract covered October Year 1 through September Year 2. No correcting entry was made. Assuming the error is never corrected, what is the effect on Year 2 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $27,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Understated by $9,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $27,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $9,000

questions.explanation: Of the $36,000 ($3,000 per month), three months (October–December Year 1 = $9,000) belong to Year 1 and nine months (January–September Year 2 = $27,000) belong to Year 2. By expensing the full $36,000 in Year 1, the $27,000 portion that should have been deferred as a prepaid asset and expensed in Year 2 was instead charged to Year 1. Because the prepaid is never set up, Year 2 records no expense for this contract even though $27,000 of service is consumed in Year 2. Year 2 expense is therefore understated by $27,000, so Year 2 net income is overstated by $27,000. This counterbalancing error reverses the Year 1 understatement: Year 1 income was understated by $27,000 and Year 2 income is overstated by the same $27,000.
questions.explanationJa: $36,000のうち（月額$3,000）3か月分（Year 1の10〜12月 = $9,000）がYear 1に、9か月分（Year 2の1〜9月 = $27,000）がYear 2に帰属する。Year 1に$36,000全額を費用処理したため、Year 2に費用化すべき$27,000がYear 1に賦課された。前払を計上しないため、Year 2はこの契約の費用を計上しないが、$27,000相当のサービスがYear 2に消費されている。したがってYear 2の費用は$27,000過少で、Year 2の純利益は$27,000過大。この相殺誤謬はYear 1の過少（$27,000）を反転させ、Year 2の利益が同額$27,000過大となる。

questions.stem: Delmar Inc. changes from the weighted-average method to FIFO for inventory on January 1, Year 4, and presents Years 3 and 4 comparatively. Under FIFO, inventory would have been higher than under weighted average by $150,000 at December 31, Year 2 and by $210,000 at December 31, Year 3. The tax rate is 20%. What is the adjustment to retained earnings at the beginning of Year 3 (the earliest period presented)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $168,000 increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: $150,000 increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: $120,000 increase

choices.label: D
choices.text: $48,000 increase

questions.explanation: Retrospective application adjusts opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented (beginning of Year 3, which equals the December 31, Year 2 balance). The cumulative pre-tax difference at that date is the $150,000 by which FIFO inventory exceeds weighted-average inventory, which increases prior income. After tax = $150,000 × (1 − 0.20) = $120,000 increase to beginning retained earnings of Year 3. The $210,000 figure relates to the end of Year 3 and affects the restated Year 3 income statement, not the opening balance.
questions.explanationJa: 遡及適用は表示される最も古い期間の期首利益剰余金（Year 3期首 = Year 2末残高）を調整する。その時点の累積税引前差額は、FIFO棚卸資産が加重平均棚卸資産を上回る$150,000であり、過年度の利益を増加させる。税引後 = $150,000 ×（1 − 0.20）= Year 3期首利益剰余金の$120,000増加。$210,000はYear 3末に関するもので、修正再表示後のYear 3損益計算書に影響し、期首残高には影響しない。

questions.stem: Marlow Co. borrowed $600,000 on November 1, Year 1, at 9% annual interest, with interest payable annually. The company failed to accrue interest at December 31, Year 1, and recorded the full year of interest as expense when paid on October 31, Year 2. Ignoring taxes, what is the effect of this error on Year 1 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $9,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $54,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $9,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $4,500

questions.explanation: Interest accruing from November 1 to December 31, Year 1 (2 months) = $600,000 × 9% × 2/12 = $9,000. This amount should have been recorded as Year 1 interest expense and accrued interest payable. By failing to accrue it, Year 1 expense was understated by $9,000, so Year 1 net income was overstated by $9,000. When the full $54,000 annual interest is paid in Year 2, $9,000 of it actually belongs to Year 1.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1の11月1日から12月31日まで（2か月）の発生利息 = $600,000 × 9% × 2/12 = $9,000。この金額をYear 1の利息費用および未払利息として計上すべきであった。未計上のため、Year 1の費用が$9,000過少となり、Year 1の純利益が$9,000過大。Year 2に年間利息$54,000全額を支払う際、そのうち$9,000は実際にはYear 1に帰属する。

questions.subtopic: Change in Depreciation Method - DDB to Straight-Line
questions.stem: Ashford Inc. acquired equipment on January 1, Year 1, for $300,000 with a 5-year life and no residual value, depreciated using double-declining balance. On January 1, Year 3, the company switched to straight-line depreciation over the remaining life. What is the depreciation expense for Year 3?
questions.explanation: The DDB rate = 2/5 = 40%. Year 1 depreciation = $300,000 × 40% = $120,000, leaving a carrying amount of $180,000. Year 2 depreciation = $180,000 × 40% = $72,000, leaving a carrying amount of $108,000 at the end of Year 2. The switch to straight-line is a change in estimate effected by a change in principle, applied prospectively. The remaining carrying amount of $108,000 (no residual) is spread over the remaining 3 years: $108,000 / 3 = $36,000.
questions.explanationJa: DDB法の償却率 = 2/5 = 40%。Year 1の減価償却 = $300,000 × 40% = $120,000で、帳簿価額は$180,000。Year 2の減価償却 = $180,000 × 40% = $72,000で、Year 2末の帳簿価額は$108,000。定額法への変更は方針変更を伴う見積りの変更であり、将来に向かって適用する。残存帳簿価額$108,000（残存価額なし）を残り3年で配分：$108,000 / 3 = $36,000。

questions.subtopic: Non-Counterbalancing Error - Depreciation on Expensed Asset
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Brighton Co. paid $180,000 to overhaul a machine, a cost that should have been capitalized and depreciated straight-line over 6 years with no residual value. Instead, the entire amount was expensed in Year 1. The error is discovered at the end of Year 3. Ignoring taxes, by what amount is retained earnings misstated at December 31, Year 3, before correction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Understated by $90,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Understated by $180,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $120,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $90,000

questions.explanation: The $180,000 should have been capitalized and depreciated at $30,000 per year ($180,000 / 6). Through Year 3 (three years), correct cumulative depreciation = $30,000 × 3 = $90,000, so the correct carrying amount is $180,000 − $90,000 = $90,000. Because the entire $180,000 was expensed in Year 1, the asset's carrying amount on the books is $0. Retained earnings is understated by the difference between what should remain on the books and what was recorded: $90,000. The asset is also understated by $90,000.
questions.explanationJa: $180,000は資産計上し年$30,000（$180,000 / 6）で減価償却すべきであった。Year 3まで（3年間）の正しい累計減価償却 = $30,000 × 3 = $90,000で、正しい帳簿価額は$180,000 − $90,000 = $90,000。$180,000全額をYear 1に費用処理したため、帳簿上の資産の帳簿価額は$0。利益剰余金は帳簿に残すべき額と計上額との差額$90,000だけ過少。資産も$90,000過少。

questions.subtopic: Change in Accounting Principle - Mandatory Adoption
questions.stem: Under ASC 250, when a newly issued FASB Accounting Standards Update mandates a specific transition method for adopting the new standard, how does an entity apply the change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It must always apply the new standard retrospectively regardless of the Update's provisions

choices.label: B
choices.text: It follows the transition guidance specified in the new Update; only in the absence of such guidance does the general retrospective rule apply

choices.label: C
choices.text: It always applies the change prospectively because the change was externally mandated

choices.label: D
choices.text: It records the entire effect as a cumulative adjustment in current-period earnings

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-2 distinguishes between mandated and voluntary changes. When a newly issued Accounting Standards Update includes specific transition provisions, the entity applies those provisions, which may be full retrospective, modified retrospective, or prospective. The general requirement of retrospective application in ASC 250 applies only when the new pronouncement does not specify its own transition method. The transition approach is therefore driven by the Update itself, not a single fixed rule.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-2は強制的変更と任意の変更を区別する。新たに公表された会計基準アップデートが特定の移行規定を含む場合、企業はその規定（完全遡及、修正遡及、または将来適用のいずれか）に従う。ASC 250の遡及適用という一般要件は、新基準が独自の移行方法を定めていない場合にのみ適用される。したがって移行方法は単一の固定ルールではなく、アップデート自体によって決まる。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Depreciation Overstated on Useful Life
questions.stem: Caldwell Corp. purchased a building on January 1, Year 1, for $1,200,000 and depreciated it straight-line over 20 years with no residual value. At the end of Year 4, the company discovered it had erroneously used a 15-year life in its records. Ignoring taxes, what is the prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings at January 1, Year 4 to correct the error?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $60,000 increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: $80,000 increase

choices.label: C
choices.text: $60,000 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: $20,000 increase

questions.explanation: Correct depreciation = $1,200,000 / 20 = $60,000 per year. Erroneous depreciation = $1,200,000 / 15 = $80,000 per year. The error overstated depreciation by $20,000 each year. Through Year 3 (the years before the earliest affected opening balance at January 1, Year 4), the cumulative overstatement = $20,000 × 3 = $60,000. Overstated expense understated retained earnings, so the correction increases beginning retained earnings of Year 4 by $60,000. Year 4 itself uses the correct $60,000 going forward.
questions.explanationJa: 正しい減価償却 = $1,200,000 / 20 = 年$60,000。誤った減価償却 = $1,200,000 / 15 = 年$80,000。誤りにより毎年$20,000の償却過大。Year 3まで（Year 4期首残高に影響する誤りの累積）の過大計上累計 = $20,000 × 3 = $60,000。費用の過大計上は利益剰余金を過少にするため、訂正によりYear 4期首利益剰余金が$60,000増加する。Year 4以降は正しい$60,000を使用する。

questions.stem: Sentinel Co. purchased a machine on January 1, Year 1, for $500,000 with a $20,000 residual value, depreciated using the units-of-production method based on an originally estimated total output of 240,000 units. The machine produced 40,000 units in Year 1 and 50,000 units in Year 2. On January 1, Year 3, the company revised the estimated remaining output to 100,000 units (residual value unchanged). The machine produced 30,000 units in Year 3. What is the depreciation expense for Year 3?
questions.explanation: Original depreciation per unit = ($500,000 − $20,000) / 240,000 = $2.00. Depreciation recorded = (40,000 + 50,000) × $2.00 = $180,000, leaving a carrying amount of $500,000 − $180,000 = $320,000 at January 1, Year 3. A revision of the estimated remaining output is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively. The revised rate spreads the remaining depreciable base over the revised remaining output: ($320,000 − $20,000) / 100,000 = $3.00 per unit. Year 3 depreciation = 30,000 units × $3.00 = $90,000.
questions.explanationJa: 当初の単位当たり減価償却 =（$500,000 − $20,000）/ 240,000 = $2.00。計上済減価償却 =（40,000 + 50,000）× $2.00 = $180,000で、Year 3期首の帳簿価額は $500,000 − $180,000 = $320,000。残存生産量の見積りの改訂は将来に向かって適用する見積りの変更。改訂後の率は残存償却対象額を改訂後の残存生産量に配分する：（$320,000 − $20,000）/ 100,000 = 1単位$3.00。Year 3の減価償却 = 30,000単位 × $3.00 = $90,000。

questions.stem: On December 1, Year 1, Foster Co. received $48,000 cash for services to be performed evenly over the following four months and recorded the entire amount as revenue in Year 1. No adjusting entry was made. Ignoring taxes, what is the effect of this error on Year 1 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $36,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Understated by $36,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Overstated by $12,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $48,000

questions.explanation: The $48,000 covers four months at $12,000 per month. Only December Year 1 ($12,000) is earned in Year 1; the remaining three months ($36,000) are earned in Year 2 and should have been recorded as unearned (deferred) revenue at December 31, Year 1. By recognizing the entire $48,000 as Year 1 revenue, revenue and net income were overstated by $36,000 in Year 1.
questions.explanationJa: $48,000は4か月分、月額$12,000をカバーする。Year 1に稼得されるのはYear 1の12月分（$12,000）のみで、残り3か月分（$36,000）はYear 2に稼得され、12月31日時点で前受（繰延）収益として計上すべきであった。$48,000全額をYear 1の収益として認識したため、Year 1の収益と純利益が$36,000過大。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Depletion Rate Revision
questions.stem: Granite Mining Co. purchased a mineral deposit for $5,000,000, originally estimated to contain 1,000,000 tons. After extracting 300,000 tons over the first two years, geologists revised the total recoverable estimate to 700,000 tons remaining (from the point of revision). What is the depletion rate per ton applied prospectively after the revision?
questions.explanation: Original depletion rate = $5,000,000 / 1,000,000 tons = $5.00 per ton. Depletion recorded for 300,000 tons extracted = 300,000 × $5.00 = $1,500,000. Remaining depletable cost = $5,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $3,500,000. A revision of recoverable reserves is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively. The revised rate = $3,500,000 / 700,000 tons remaining = $5.00 per ton. The rate remains $5.00 because the remaining cost and revised remaining tons are proportionate.
questions.explanationJa: 当初の減耗償却率 = $5,000,000 / 1,000,000トン = 1トン当たり$5.00。採掘済300,000トンの減耗償却 = 300,000 × $5.00 = $1,500,000。残存償却対象原価 = $5,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $3,500,000。回収可能埋蔵量の改訂は将来に向かって適用する見積りの変更。改訂後の率 = $3,500,000 / 残り700,000トン = 1トン当たり$5.00。残存原価と改訂後の残存トン数が比例するため、率は$5.00のままとなる。

questions.stem: In Year 3, before issuing its Year 3 statements, Hadley Inc. discovered that it had failed to record $40,000 of depreciation in Year 2. Year 2 statements (already issued) are presented comparatively with Year 3. Ignoring taxes, how should this error be reflected in the comparative financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase Year 3 depreciation expense by $40,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the comparative Year 2 income statement to add $40,000 of depreciation expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Adjust the beginning retained earnings of Year 2 by $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the error in the notes without any statement adjustment

questions.explanation: The omitted depreciation belongs to Year 2, which is presented comparatively. Under ASC 250-10-45-23, the error is corrected by restating the period in which it occurred. The Year 2 comparative income statement is restated to include the additional $40,000 depreciation expense, reducing Year 2 net income. Because the error originated in Year 2 (a period presented), the adjustment flows through the restated Year 2 income statement rather than through opening retained earnings of Year 2 or through Year 3 expense.
questions.explanationJa: 計上漏れの減価償却はYear 2に帰属し、Year 2は比較表示される。ASC 250-10-45-23に基づき、誤謬は発生した期間を修正再表示して訂正する。Year 2の比較損益計算書を修正再表示して$40,000の追加減価償却費を含め、Year 2の純利益を減少させる。誤謬はYear 2（表示される期間）で生じたため、調整はYear 2の期首利益剰余金やYear 3の費用ではなく、修正再表示後のYear 2損益計算書を通じて反映される。

questions.subtopic: Change in Principle - Inseparable from Estimate
questions.stem: When a change in accounting principle is inseparable from the effect of a change in accounting estimate, ASC 250 requires the change to be accounted for as a:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Change in accounting principle, applied retrospectively

choices.label: B
choices.text: Correction of an error, applied as a prior period adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Change in accounting estimate, applied prospectively

choices.label: D
choices.text: Change in reporting entity, with restatement of all prior periods

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-18 states that a change in accounting estimate that is effected by a change in accounting principle—where the effect of the principle change is inseparable from the effect of the estimate change—is accounted for as a change in accounting estimate. It is therefore applied prospectively. The most common example is a change in depreciation, amortization, or depletion method, which is treated prospectively rather than retrospectively.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-18は、会計方針の変更を伴う会計上の見積りの変更（方針変更の影響が見積り変更の影響と不可分な場合）を、会計上の見積りの変更として処理すると定める。したがって将来に向かって適用する。最も一般的な例は減価償却・償却・減耗償却方法の変更であり、遡及ではなく将来に向かって処理される。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Overstated Ending Inventory Two-Year Net
questions.stem: Larkspur Co. overstated its Year 1 ending inventory by $55,000 and understated its Year 2 ending inventory by $30,000. Neither error was corrected. Ignoring taxes, what is the net effect of these two errors on the December 31, Year 2 retained earnings balance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $25,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $85,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $30,000

questions.explanation: The Year 1 ending inventory overstatement of $55,000 is a counterbalancing error: it overstated Year 1 income and understated Year 2 income by the same $55,000, so by December 31, Year 2 its cumulative effect on retained earnings is zero. The Year 2 ending inventory understatement of $30,000 understated Year 2 income (overstated cost of goods sold) and has not yet counterbalanced. At December 31, Year 2, retained earnings is therefore understated by $30,000, driven entirely by the open Year 2 inventory error.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1期末棚卸の$55,000過大計上は相殺誤謬：Year 1の利益を過大、Year 2の利益を同額$55,000過少にするため、Year 2末時点で利益剰余金への累積影響はゼロ。Year 2期末棚卸の$30,000過少計上はYear 2の利益を過少（売上原価を過大）にし、まだ相殺されていない。したがってYear 2末の利益剰余金は、未相殺のYear 2棚卸誤謬により$30,000過少。

questions.subtopic: Change in Reporting Entity - Pooling of Subsidiaries
questions.stem: Under ASC 250, which characteristic best identifies a change in reporting entity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The current-period financial statements are, in effect, those of a different group of entities than in prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: A mathematical mistake was made in a prior period's consolidation worksheet

choices.label: C
choices.text: Management revised the estimated useful lives of consolidated fixed assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity adopted a newly issued revenue recognition standard

questions.explanation: A change in reporting entity occurs when the financial statements being presented are, in substance, those of a different reporting entity than in prior periods—for example, presenting consolidated statements in place of individual statements or changing the specific subsidiaries comprising the group. It is reported retrospectively. A mathematical consolidation mistake is an error correction, revising useful lives is a change in estimate, and adopting a new standard is a change in principle—none of which is a change in reporting entity.
questions.explanationJa: 報告企業の変更は、表示される財務諸表が実質的に過年度とは異なる報告企業のものとなる場合に生じる。例えば、個別財務諸表に代えて連結財務諸表を表示する、またはグループを構成する特定の子会社を変更する場合である。遡及的に報告される。連結の計算ミスは誤謬の訂正、耐用年数の改訂は見積りの変更、新基準の採用は方針の変更であり、いずれも報告企業の変更ではない。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Westbrook Co. constructed a qualifying asset and should have capitalized $48,000 of interest into its cost, but instead expensed the full amount. The asset was placed in service on January 1, Year 2 and is depreciated straight-line over 8 years with no residual value. The error is discovered at the end of Year 2. Ignoring taxes, what is the combined effect of correcting the error on the December 31, Year 2 retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $42,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase of $48,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase of $6,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase of $42,000

questions.explanation: In Year 1, expensing $48,000 of interest that should have been capitalized understated Year 1 income by $48,000 (retained earnings understated by $48,000). Once capitalized, the $48,000 increases the asset cost and is depreciated over 8 years beginning Year 2: $48,000 / 8 = $6,000 of additional Year 2 depreciation, which reduces Year 2 income by $6,000. The net cumulative correction to December 31, Year 2 retained earnings = $48,000 − $6,000 = $42,000 increase.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1に資産化すべき利息$48,000を費用処理したため、Year 1の利益が$48,000過少（利益剰余金$48,000過少）。資産化すると$48,000が資産原価を増やし、Year 2から8年で償却される：$48,000 / 8 = $6,000のYear 2追加減価償却で、Year 2の利益が$6,000減少。Year 2末利益剰余金への純累積訂正 = $48,000 − $6,000 = $42,000の増加。

questions.stem: Pemberton Co. estimates annual depreciation at the start of each year. In the third quarter of Year 5, it revises the remaining useful life of a machine, increasing the annual depreciation from $80,000 to $120,000. The first two quarters were recorded at the old rate. How is the change reflected in the third-quarter interim statements under ASC 270?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate the first two quarters to the new rate and record a cumulative catch-up in Q3

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record depreciation in Q3 and future quarters at the revised rate, with no restatement of Q1 and Q2

choices.label: C
choices.text: Spread the full $120,000 evenly over all four quarters retroactively

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer the entire effect of the change to the fourth quarter

questions.explanation: Under ASC 270-10-45-14 and ASC 250-10-45-17, a change in accounting estimate is accounted for in the period of change and future periods; it is never applied retrospectively. The first two quarters of Year 5 are not restated. Depreciation for the third and fourth quarters reflects the revised annual rate, so the remaining annual amount is recognized over the remaining interim periods of the year going forward. No cumulative catch-up is recorded for the quarters already reported.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 270-10-45-14およびASC 250-10-45-17に基づき、会計上の見積りの変更は変更期間および将来の期間に処理し、遡及適用は決して行わない。Year 5の最初の2四半期は修正再表示しない。第3・第4四半期の減価償却は改訂後の年間率を反映し、残りの年間額を当年度の残りの中間期間にわたり将来に向かって認識する。既に報告済みの四半期についてキャッチアップ調整は計上しない。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Cash to Accrual Conversion
questions.stem: Hollis Co. has kept its books on the cash basis and is converting to accrual at the beginning of Year 5. At that date, accounts receivable are $130,000, accounts payable are $45,000, accrued wages payable are $18,000, and prepaid rent is $9,000. Ignoring taxes, what is the net adjustment to retained earnings to convert to the accrual basis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit (increase) of $94,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Credit (increase) of $130,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit (decrease) of $76,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Credit (increase) of $76,000

questions.explanation: Converting from cash to accrual: receivables of $130,000 add unrecorded revenue (increase retained earnings); prepaid rent of $9,000 is an asset whose expense was prematurely recognized under cash basis (increase retained earnings); accounts payable of $45,000 add unrecorded expense (decrease retained earnings); accrued wages payable of $18,000 add unrecorded expense (decrease retained earnings). Net = $130,000 + $9,000 − $45,000 − $18,000 = $76,000 credit (increase) to retained earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 現金主義から発生主義への転換：売掛金$130,000は未計上の収益を加算（利益剰余金増加）、前払家賃$9,000は現金主義で早期に費用認識された資産（利益剰余金増加）、買掛金$45,000は未計上の費用を加算（利益剰余金減少）、未払賃金$18,000は未計上の費用を加算（利益剰余金減少）。純額 = $130,000 + $9,000 − $45,000 − $18,000 = $76,000の利益剰余金の貸方（増加）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, disclosure of the effect of a change in accounting estimate on income from continuing operations, net income, and related per-share amounts is required:

choices.label: A
choices.text: For every change in estimate, regardless of magnitude

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only for changes in estimate that affect several future periods, if the effect is material

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only when the change decreases net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only when mandated by the SEC for public companies

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-50-4 requires disclosure of the effect on income from continuing operations, net income, and per-share amounts for a change in estimate that affects several future periods (such as a change in service lives of depreciable assets), provided the effect is material. Disclosure of the effect is not required for changes in estimate made each period in the ordinary course of business (for example, uncollectible accounts) unless the effect is material. The requirement is not triggered by the direction of the income effect or limited to SEC mandates.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-50-4は、複数の将来期間に影響する見積りの変更（償却資産の耐用年数の変更など）について、影響が重要である場合に、継続事業利益・当期純利益・1株当たり金額への影響の開示を要求する。通常の営業の過程で各期間に行われる見積りの変更（貸倒など）については、影響が重要でない限り開示は不要。要件は利益への影響の方向によって生じるものでも、SECの要求に限定されるものでもない。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Gain on Sale Misclassification
questions.stem: In Year 1, Sterling Co. sold land with a carrying amount of $200,000 for $260,000 cash but erroneously credited the entire $260,000 proceeds to a revenue account, recording no cost of the land sold. The error is discovered in Year 2 after the Year 1 statements were issued. Ignoring taxes, what is the prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings of Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $200,000 increase

choices.label: B
choices.text: $60,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $260,000 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: $200,000 decrease

questions.explanation: The correct gain on the sale is proceeds $260,000 − carrying amount $200,000 = $60,000. The company instead recorded $260,000 of income and left the $200,000 land still on the books, overstating Year 1 income by $200,000 (the cost of the land that was never removed). The land balance is also overstated by $200,000. The correcting prior period adjustment removes the land and reduces beginning retained earnings of Year 2 by $200,000.
questions.explanationJa: 売却の正しい利得は売却代金$260,000 − 帳簿価額$200,000 = $60,000。会社は代わりに$260,000の収益を計上し、$200,000の土地を帳簿に残したため、Year 1の利益が$200,000（除却されなかった土地の原価）過大。土地残高も$200,000過大。訂正の前期修正により土地を除却し、Year 2期首利益剰余金を$200,000減少させる。

questions.subtopic: Change in Principle - Direct Effect Identification
questions.stem: Which of the following is a direct effect of a change in accounting principle that is recognized through retrospective application under ASC 250?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in an employee bonus accrual that is contractually based on reported net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: The adjustment to the inventory carrying amount resulting from switching costing methods, and the related deferred tax effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in a royalty payment computed as a percentage of reported sales

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in a contractual profit-sharing distribution triggered by the restated income

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-5 and 45-8 distinguish direct effects from indirect effects. A direct effect is a change required to restate prior-period financial statements to apply the new principle—such as the adjustment to inventory carrying amounts from switching costing methods and the related deferred income tax effect. These are applied retrospectively. Bonus accruals, royalties, and profit-sharing distributions that are based on reported amounts are indirect effects, recognized in the period of change rather than retrospectively.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-5および45-8は直接的影響と間接的影響を区別する。直接的影響は、新方針を適用するために過年度財務諸表を修正再表示するのに必要な変更であり、原価計算方法の変更による棚卸資産帳簿価額の調整とその関連繰延税金影響などである。これらは遡及適用される。報告額に基づく賞与引当、ロイヤルティ、利益分配は間接的影響であり、遡及ではなく変更期間に認識される。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Self-Correcting Over Two Years
questions.stem: Yardley Co. failed to record accrued utilities payable of $14,000 at December 31, Year 1; the utilities were paid and fully expensed in Year 2. The error was not discovered until after the Year 2 books were closed. By the end of Year 2, before any correction, the December 31, Year 2 retained earnings balance is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Understated by $14,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $14,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Correctly stated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $28,000

questions.explanation: Failing to accrue the $14,000 at the end of Year 1 understated Year 1 expense, overstating Year 1 income by $14,000. When the utilities were paid and expensed in Year 2, the same $14,000 hit Year 2 expense even though it economically belonged to Year 1, understating Year 2 income by $14,000. The overstatement in Year 1 and the understatement in Year 2 offset exactly, so by December 31, Year 2 the accumulated effect on retained earnings is zero and the balance is correctly stated. This is a counterbalancing error.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1末に$14,000を未計上にしたため、Year 1の費用が過少となりYear 1の利益が$14,000過大。Year 2に公共料金を支払い費用処理した際、経済的にはYear 1に帰属する同じ$14,000がYear 2の費用となり、Year 2の利益が$14,000過少。Year 1の過大とYear 2の過少が正確に相殺されるため、Year 2末時点で利益剰余金への累積影響はゼロで、残高は正しく表示される。これは相殺誤謬である。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Inventory Cutoff (Goods in Transit FOB Shipping Point)
questions.stem: On December 31, Year 1, Tilden Co. purchased goods costing $70,000 shipped FOB shipping point that were still in transit at year-end. Tilden neither included the goods in its ending physical inventory count nor recorded the purchase. The goods were received and the purchase recorded in January Year 2. The company uses the periodic inventory system. Ignoring taxes, what is the effect of this error on the Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income is correctly stated; assets and liabilities are both understated by $70,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net income is overstated by $70,000 and liabilities are understated by $70,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net income is understated by $70,000 and inventory is overstated by $70,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net income is overstated by $70,000 and assets are overstated by $70,000

questions.explanation: Under FOB shipping point, title passes to the buyer when the goods are shipped, so the in-transit goods belong to Tilden at December 31, Year 1 and should appear in both ending inventory and accounts payable. Neither was recorded: ending inventory is understated by $70,000 and purchases (and accounts payable) are understated by $70,000. Under the periodic system, cost of goods sold = beginning inventory + purchases − ending inventory. Purchases understated by $70,000 lowers cost of goods sold by $70,000, while ending inventory understated by $70,000 raises cost of goods sold by $70,000; the two effects exactly offset, so cost of goods sold and net income are correctly stated. The balance sheet, however, understates inventory (assets) by $70,000 and accounts payable (liabilities) by $70,000.
questions.explanationJa: FOB船積地条件では商品の船積時に所有権が買手に移転するため、輸送中の商品は12月31日時点でTilden社に帰属し、期末棚卸資産と買掛金の両方に計上すべきである。いずれも未計上であり、期末棚卸資産が$70,000過少、仕入（および買掛金）も$70,000過少。定期棚卸法では、売上原価 = 期首棚卸 + 仕入 − 期末棚卸。仕入の$70,000過少は売上原価を$70,000減少させ、期末棚卸の$70,000過少は売上原価を$70,000増加させるため、両者は正確に相殺し、売上原価と純利益は正しく表示される。ただし貸借対照表上、棚卸資産（資産）が$70,000過少、買掛金（負債）も$70,000過少となる。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Depreciation on Overstated Cost
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Ridgeway Co. capitalized routine repair costs of $60,000 as part of equipment cost. The amount should have been expensed when incurred. The equipment is depreciated straight-line over 5 years with no residual value. The error is discovered at the end of Year 2. Ignoring taxes, what is the net effect of correcting the error on the December 31, Year 2 retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $12,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $60,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease of $24,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Decrease of $36,000

questions.explanation: The $60,000 should have been expensed in Year 1, reducing Year 1 income by $60,000. Because it was wrongly capitalized, the company instead recorded depreciation of $60,000 / 5 = $12,000 per year. Over Year 1 and Year 2, depreciation of $24,000 ($12,000 × 2) was recorded on the improperly capitalized cost. Correcting the error removes the remaining $36,000 of capitalized cost from the asset and reduces retained earnings by that amount: the $60,000 that should have been expensed, less the $24,000 already charged to income through depreciation = $36,000 net decrease.
questions.explanationJa: $60,000はYear 1に費用処理すべきで、Year 1の利益を$60,000減少させるはずであった。誤って資産化したため、会社は代わりに$60,000 / 5 = 年$12,000の減価償却を計上した。Year 1とYear 2で、不適切に資産化した原価に$24,000（$12,000 × 2）の減価償却が計上された。誤謬を訂正すると、資産から残りの資産化原価$36,000を除却し、その分だけ利益剰余金を減少させる：費用処理すべきだった$60,000から、減価償却で既に利益に賦課された$24,000を差し引いた$36,000の純減少。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Combined With Additional Cost
questions.stem: Oakdale Co. owns a machine purchased for $400,000 on January 1, Year 1, with a 10-year life and no residual value (straight-line). On January 1, Year 6, the company spent $90,000 on a major upgrade that did not extend the original useful life but improved output. What is the depreciation expense for Year 6?
questions.explanation: Original annual depreciation = $400,000 / 10 = $40,000. Accumulated depreciation through Year 5 = $40,000 × 5 = $200,000; carrying amount at January 1, Year 6 = $400,000 − $200,000 = $200,000. The $90,000 upgrade is capitalized, raising the depreciable base to $200,000 + $90,000 = $290,000. The remaining useful life is unchanged at 10 − 5 = 5 years. Depreciating this revised base prospectively (a change in estimate): $290,000 / 5 = $58,000 for Year 6.
questions.explanationJa: 当初の年間減価償却 = $400,000 / 10 = $40,000。Year 5末までの累計減価償却 = $40,000 × 5 = $200,000、Year 6期首の帳簿価額 = $400,000 − $200,000 = $200,000。$90,000の改良は資産化され、償却対象額を$200,000 + $90,000 = $290,000に引き上げる。残存耐用年数は10 − 5 = 5年で変わらない。この改訂後の額を将来に向かって償却（見積りの変更）：$290,000 / 5 = Year 6は$58,000。

questions.subtopic: Error vs Change in Estimate Classification
questions.stem: A company recognizes a material adjustment in the current year. Which of the following situations represents the correction of an error rather than a change in accounting estimate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increasing the allowance for doubtful accounts because of deteriorating economic conditions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Shortening the estimated useful life of equipment based on new usage data

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recording inventory that was on hand at year-end but omitted from the prior year's physical count due to an oversight

choices.label: D
choices.text: Revising the estimated warranty liability percentage using updated claim history

questions.explanation: Omitting inventory that was physically on hand from a prior year's count is a mistake in the use of facts that existed at the reporting date; under ASC 250 it is the correction of an error, requiring restatement of the prior period. The other situations involve revising estimates based on new information that became available after the original estimates were made—increasing the doubtful accounts allowance, shortening a useful life, and updating a warranty percentage—each of which is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively.
questions.explanationJa: 過年度の棚卸でその場にあった在庫を計上漏れすることは、報告日に存在した事実の使用における誤りであり、ASC 250に基づき誤謬の訂正に該当し、過年度の修正再表示を要する。他の状況は、当初の見積り後に入手可能となった新情報に基づく見積りの改訂であり（貸倒引当金の増加、耐用年数の短縮、保証率の更新）、いずれも将来に向かって適用する会計上の見積りの変更である。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Holloway Corp. voluntarily changed from FIFO to the weighted-average method for inventory. Net income under each method was: Year 1 — FIFO $500,000, weighted average $460,000; Year 2 — FIFO $620,000, weighted average $600,000. Holloway presents comparative financial statements for Year 2 and Year 3. Ignoring income taxes, what adjustment should be made to the beginning retained earnings of Year 2, the earliest period presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $60,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $40,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $20,000 decrease

questions.explanation: Under retrospective application, the cumulative effect of the change on periods BEFORE the earliest period presented is recorded as an adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented (Year 2). Only Year 1 precedes Year 2, and the Year 1 difference is $500,000 − $460,000 = $40,000 lower income under weighted average, so beginning Year 2 retained earnings decreases by $40,000. The Year 2 difference of $20,000 is reflected by restating the Year 2 income statement itself, not the opening balance; combining both years ($60,000) would double-count the Year 2 effect, and a zero adjustment would ignore the required retrospective treatment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-5に基づき、会計方針の変更は遡及適用され、表示される最も早い期間（Year 2）より前の期間に係る累積的影響額は、その最も早い期間の期首利益剰余金の修正として計上する。Year 2より前はYear 1のみであり、その差額は $500,000 − $460,000 = $40,000（加重平均法の方が利益が少ない）。よってYear 2期首利益剰余金を$40,000減額する。Year 2の差額$20,000はYear 2の損益計算書自体を修正再表示することで反映されるため、期首残高調整には含めない。両年度合計の$60,000とするとYear 2の影響を二重計上することになる。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Useful Life Extension
questions.stem: Calloway Co. purchased equipment on January 1, Year 1, for $720,000 with no salvage value and an estimated useful life of 8 years, depreciated on a straight-line basis. At the beginning of Year 4, Calloway determined that the equipment's total useful life is 12 years (9 years remaining). What is depreciation expense for Year 4?
questions.explanation: A revision of useful life is a change in accounting estimate accounted for prospectively. Accumulated depreciation through Year 3 is ($720,000 ÷ 8) × 3 = $270,000, leaving a carrying amount of $450,000. That carrying amount is depreciated over the 9 remaining years: $450,000 ÷ 9 = $50,000. Dividing the original cost by the new 12-year life ($60,000) ignores depreciation already taken; $90,000 continues the old rate; and $37,500 incorrectly spreads the carrying amount over 12 years instead of the 9 years remaining.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数の見直しは見積りの変更であり、ASC 250-10-45-17に従い将来に向かって（prospectively）処理する。Year 3末までの減価償却累計額は ($720,000 ÷ 8年) × 3年 = $270,000、帳簿価額は $450,000。これを残存耐用年数9年で償却するため、Year 4の減価償却費は $450,000 ÷ 9年 = $50,000。取得原価を新耐用年数12年で割る$60,000は既償却分を無視しており、$90,000は旧償却率の継続、$37,500は帳簿価額を残存9年でなく12年で除した誤りである。

questions.stem: Drummond Inc. changes its inventory cost flow assumption from FIFO to LIFO. In practice, how is this change usually accounted for, and why?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively, because all changes in inventory method must restate prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a cumulative-effect adjustment reported in current period net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Prospectively, because reconstructing prior-year LIFO layers is generally impracticable, so the opening inventory becomes the first LIFO layer

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a correction of an error, with a prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings

questions.explanation: A change in accounting principle normally requires retrospective application, but ASC 250 provides an impracticability exception. A change to LIFO typically qualifies because the entity usually cannot reconstruct the LIFO cost layers that would have existed in prior years without using hindsight. Therefore the change is applied prospectively: the beginning inventory of the year of change becomes the base-year LIFO layer. Reporting a cumulative-effect adjustment in current income is the superseded APB 20 approach, and treating the change as an error correction is wrong because adopting LIFO is not the correction of a misstatement.
questions.explanationJa: 会計方針の変更は原則遡及適用だが、ASC 250-10-45-9は遡及適用が実務上不可能（impracticable）な場合の例外を認めている。LIFOへの変更では、過年度に存在したはずのLIFO原価層（レイヤー）を事後的判断なしに再構築することが通常不可能なため、この例外に該当する典型例である。よって変更年度の期首棚卸資産を基準層（base layer）として将来に向かって適用する。累積的影響額を当期純利益に計上する処理は廃止された旧APB 20の方法であり、誤謬訂正として処理するのも誤り（LIFO採用は誤謬の訂正ではない）。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Whitfield Co. issued $2,000,000 of 10-year bonds for $1,940,000 and properly recorded the $60,000 discount. However, Whitfield recorded only the cash interest each year and never amortized the discount (straight-line amortization is appropriate and not materially different from the effective interest method). The error is discovered at the beginning of Year 3. Ignoring income taxes, beginning Year 3 retained earnings is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Understated by $12,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $6,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $6,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $12,000

questions.explanation: Straight-line discount amortization is $60,000 ÷ 10 years = $6,000 per year, which increases interest expense above the cash interest paid. Omitting it understated interest expense by $6,000 in Year 1 and $6,000 in Year 2, overstating cumulative income — and therefore beginning Year 3 retained earnings — by $12,000. An understatement direction is backwards (discount amortization adds to expense, so omitting it inflates income), and $6,000 captures only one of the two affected years.
questions.explanationJa: 定額法による割引額（ディスカウント）の償却は $60,000 ÷ 10年 = 年$6,000 で、現金支払利息に加算して支払利息を増加させる。これを2年間（Year 1・Year 2）失念したため、支払利息が累計 $12,000 過小計上され、利益およびYear 3期首利益剰余金は $12,000 過大となっている。ASC 250-10-45-23により、誤謬訂正として期首利益剰余金を減額する修正再表示が必要。過小（understated）方向の選択は割引償却が費用を増やすという関係を逆に捉えた誤りで、$6,000は1年分しか考慮していない。

questions.subtopic: Interim Reporting - Change in Principle During the Year
questions.stem: In the third quarter of Year 2, Lakemont Corp. voluntarily changes from the average cost method to FIFO for inventory. How should this change in accounting principle be reflected in Lakemont's interim financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Apply the new method beginning in the third quarter only, with no adjustment to earlier quarters

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the change retrospectively, restating previously issued interim statements of the current year and prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: Report the cumulative effect of the change in third-quarter net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer recognition of the change until the annual financial statements are issued

questions.explanation: ASC 250 requires that a change in accounting principle made in an interim period be applied retrospectively, the same as an annual change: previously issued interim statements of the current fiscal year are restated to reflect the new principle, along with prior annual periods presented. Applying the new method only from the quarter of change is a prospective treatment reserved for estimate changes; a cumulative-effect catch-up in current-quarter income is the superseded approach; and deferral until year-end is not permitted.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-14およびASC 270により、中間期に行われた会計方針の変更も年度の変更と同様に遡及適用しなければならない。すなわち、当年度の既発行中間財務諸表および表示される過年度を新方針で修正再表示する。変更した四半期から将来適用する処理は見積りの変更に対する取扱いであり、累積的影響額を当四半期損益に一括計上する方法は廃止された旧処理、年度末まで認識を繰り延べることも認められない。

questions.subtopic: Disclosure - Changes in Estimate Made in the Ordinary Course
questions.stem: Under ASC 250, which statement best describes the disclosure requirement for changes in accounting estimates?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All changes in estimate must be disclosed, regardless of materiality

choices.label: B
choices.text: Changes in estimate are never disclosed because they are accounted for prospectively

choices.label: C
choices.text: Changes in estimate require restated prior-period disclosures identical to those for changes in principle

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosure of the effect on income from continuing operations, net income, and per-share amounts is required when the change is material, but estimates made each period in the ordinary course (such as uncollectible accounts) need not be disclosed unless the effect is material

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-50-4 requires disclosure of the effect of a change in estimate on income from continuing operations, net income, and related per-share amounts of the current period when the change is material. However, disclosure is not required for estimate revisions made each period in the ordinary course of business — such as uncollectible accounts or inventory obsolescence — unless the effect is material. Requiring disclosure of all changes regardless of materiality overstates the rule; saying no disclosure is ever required understates it; and restated prior-period disclosures apply to retrospective principle changes, not prospective estimate changes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-50-4は、見積りの変更が重要（material）な場合、継続事業からの利益・当期純利益・1株当たり金額への影響の開示を求める。ただし、貸倒見積りや棚卸資産の陳腐化引当のように通常の業務過程で毎期行われる見積りの見直しについては、影響が重要でない限り開示不要である。重要性にかかわらず全ての変更を開示するという記述は要求を過大に述べており、開示が一切不要という記述は過小。過年度の修正再表示開示は遡及適用される方針変更に対する要求であり、将来的処理の見積り変更には適用されない。

questions.stem: In Year 2, Brennan Co. discovered that it failed to record $90,000 of revenue that was earned and should have been recognized in Year 1. Brennan's tax rate is 25%, and it presents single-year (non-comparative) financial statements. How should the correction be reported in Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment increasing beginning retained earnings by $67,500

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment increasing beginning retained earnings by $90,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: As additional revenue of $90,000 in Year 2 income

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment increasing beginning retained earnings by $22,500

questions.explanation: A material prior-year error is corrected through a prior period adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the current year, reported net of tax: $90,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $67,500 increase. The gross $90,000 ignores the related tax effect, $22,500 is only the tax portion, and recognizing the revenue in Year 2 income would misstate the current period — errors are never run through current earnings.
questions.explanationJa: 重要な過年度誤謬は、ASC 250-10-45-23に基づきprior period adjustmentとして当期の期首利益剰余金を修正する。修正額は税効果考慮後の純額で、$90,000 × (1 − 25%) = $67,500 の増額となる。$90,000は税効果を無視した総額、$22,500は税額部分のみであり、いずれも誤り。また、Year 2の収益として当期損益に計上することは当期の業績を歪めるため認められない（誤謬は当期損益を経由させない）。

questions.stem: Tremont Co. failed to accrue $8,000 of interest receivable at December 31, Year 1. The interest was received in cash in Year 2 and recorded as interest revenue at that time. If the error is never corrected, what is the effect on Year 2 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Understated by $8,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Overstated by $8,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $16,000

questions.explanation: The $8,000 of interest was earned in Year 1 but recorded as revenue in Year 2 when collected. Year 1 income was understated by $8,000, and Year 2 income is overstated by $8,000 because it includes revenue belonging to the prior year. This is a counterbalancing error: retained earnings at the end of Year 2 is correct. An understatement of Year 2 reverses the direction of the error, no effect describes ending retained earnings rather than Year 2 income, and $16,000 double-counts a single $8,000 misstatement.
questions.explanationJa: $8,000の利息はYear 1に稼得されたが、現金回収時のYear 2に収益計上された。このためYear 1の利益は$8,000過小、Year 2の利益は前期帰属の収益を含むため$8,000過大となる。これは相殺型（counterbalancing）の誤謬であり、Year 2末の利益剰余金は正しい金額に自己修正される。Year 2が過小という選択は影響の方向が逆であり、「影響なし」はYear 2末の利益剰余金についての記述であって当期純利益には当てはまらない。$16,000は単一の$8,000の誤謬を二重に数えている。

questions.stem: Norcross Inc. presents single-year (non-comparative) financial statements. On January 1 of the current year, Norcross voluntarily changed an accounting principle. The cumulative effect of the change on all prior years is a $130,000 decrease in pretax income, and the tax rate is 30%. How should the change be reflected in the current-year financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A $130,000 decrease to beginning retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: A $91,000 decrease to beginning retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: A $91,000 charge included in current-year net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: A $39,000 decrease to beginning retained earnings

questions.explanation: When only the current year is presented, retrospective application means the cumulative effect of the change on all prior periods is recorded, net of tax, as an adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the current year: $130,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $91,000 decrease. The gross $130,000 ignores the tax effect, $39,000 is only the tax effect, and charging current-year income with the cumulative effect is the superseded cumulative-effect approach prohibited by ASC 250.
questions.explanationJa: 単年度のみ表示する場合、遡及適用は「過年度全体への累積的影響額を税効果考慮後の純額で当期の期首利益剰余金に反映する」ことを意味する（ASC 250-10-45-5(c)）。計算は $130,000 × (1 − 30%) = $91,000 の減額。総額$130,000は税効果の考慮漏れ、$39,000は税効果部分のみ。累積的影響額を当期損益に計上する方法は旧基準（APB 20）の処理であり、ASC 250では禁止されている。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Patent Remaining Life
questions.stem: Ellsworth Co. acquired a patent on January 1, Year 1, for $360,000 and amortized it on a straight-line basis over its 12-year legal life. At the beginning of Year 5, due to new competing technology, Ellsworth determined that the patent's remaining useful life is only 4 years. What is amortization expense for Year 5?
questions.explanation: Amortization through Year 4 is ($360,000 ÷ 12) × 4 = $120,000, leaving a carrying amount of $240,000. The revised remaining life of 4 years is applied prospectively: $240,000 ÷ 4 = $60,000 per year. The $30,000 figure continues the original rate and ignores the revision, $45,000 spreads original cost over a total 8-year life without isolating the carrying amount and remaining period correctly ($360,000 ÷ 8), and $90,000 divides the full original cost by the 4 remaining years, ignoring amortization already taken.
questions.explanationJa: Year 4末までの償却累計額は ($360,000 ÷ 12年) × 4年 = $120,000、帳簿価額は $240,000。残存耐用年数の見直しは見積りの変更としてASC 250-10-45-17により将来に向かって処理し、Year 5の償却費は $240,000 ÷ 4年 = $60,000。$30,000は従来の償却率を継続した場合、$45,000は取得原価を合計8年で除した誤り（$360,000 ÷ 8）、$90,000は既償却分を無視して取得原価全額を残存4年で除した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Change in Reporting Entity - Excluded Transactions
questions.stem: Under ASC 250, which of the following is NOT accounted for as a change in reporting entity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The acquisition of a new subsidiary in a business combination accounted for under ASC 805

choices.label: B
choices.text: Presenting consolidated financial statements in place of statements of individual entities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Changing the specific subsidiaries that make up the group for which consolidated statements are presented

choices.label: D
choices.text: Changing the entities included in combined financial statements

questions.explanation: ASC 250 explicitly states that a business combination — the purchase of a new subsidiary — is not a change in reporting entity; it is accounted for prospectively under ASC 805 with no restatement of prior periods. The three situations that DO qualify as changes in reporting entity are presenting consolidated statements in place of individual-entity statements, changing the specific subsidiaries comprising the consolidated group, and changing the entities included in combined statements; each requires retrospective application to all periods presented.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-21は報告企業の変更に該当する3類型として、(1)個別財務諸表に代えて連結財務諸表を表示する場合、(2)連結対象の子会社の構成を変更する場合、(3)結合（combined）財務諸表に含める企業を変更する場合を挙げ、これらは表示する全期間に遡及適用する。一方、ASC 805に基づく企業結合による新規子会社の取得は報告企業の変更には該当せず、過年度の修正再表示は行わない（取得日以降の将来的処理）。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Error - Consigned Goods Included in Count
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Pinehurst Co. included in its physical inventory count $45,000 of goods held on consignment FROM Quill Corp. (Pinehurst is the consignee). No purchase was recorded. The error was not discovered, and Year 2 ending inventory was counted correctly. What is the effect of the error on Year 2 net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $45,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Overstated by $90,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $45,000

questions.explanation: Consigned goods belong to the consignor and should be excluded from the consignee's inventory. Including them overstated Year 1 ending inventory by $45,000, which understated Year 1 cost of goods sold and overstated Year 1 net income. In Year 2, the overstated ending inventory becomes overstated beginning inventory, which overstates Year 2 cost of goods sold and understates Year 2 net income by $45,000. An overstatement of Year 2 confuses it with the Year 1 effect, no effect applies only to combined two-year retained earnings, and $90,000 double-counts the single misstatement.
questions.explanationJa: 受託販売品（委託者Quill Corp.の所有物）は受託者Pinehurstの棚卸資産に含めてはならない。誤って含めたためYear 1期末棚卸資産が$45,000過大→Year 1売上原価が過小→Year 1利益が過大となった。Year 2では、この過大な期末棚卸資産が期首棚卸資産として引き継がれ、Year 2の売上原価が$45,000過大→Year 2純利益は$45,000過小となる。過大という選択はYear 1への影響との混同、「影響なし」は2年間通算の利益剰余金についてのみ正しい記述、$90,000は単一の誤謬の二重計上である。

questions.subtopic: Prior Period Adjustment - Financial Statement Presentation
questions.stem: When single-period financial statements are presented, a material error related to a prior period that is discovered in the current period should be reported in the current-period financial statements as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A separately disclosed component of current-period net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: An adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings, net of applicable income taxes

choices.label: C
choices.text: A footnote disclosure only, with no adjustment to any account balance

choices.label: D
choices.text: An adjustment to ending retained earnings reported as the last line of the income statement

questions.explanation: ASC 250 requires that the correction of a material prior period error be reported as a prior period adjustment — an adjustment, net of tax, to the opening balance of retained earnings in the statement of retained earnings (or statement of stockholders' equity). The error never flows through current-period net income, footnote disclosure alone does not correct the misstated balances, and the income statement does not end with retained earnings adjustments.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-23・45-24により、過年度の重要な誤謬の訂正はprior period adjustmentとして処理し、利益剰余金計算書（または株主資本等変動計算書）において期首利益剰余金残高を税効果考慮後の純額で修正する形で表示する。誤謬の影響を当期純利益に含めることは禁止されており、注記のみで残高を修正しない処理や、損益計算書の末尾で調整する表示も認められない。

questions.stem: Which of the following is accounted for prospectively, affecting only the current and future periods?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A voluntary change from LIFO to FIFO for inventory

choices.label: B
choices.text: Correction of a mathematical error in last year's depreciation calculation

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in depreciation method that is inseparable from a change in the estimated pattern of benefits

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in the subsidiaries included in consolidated financial statements

questions.explanation: A change in depreciation method is a change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle; because the principle change is inseparable from the estimate change, it is accounted for prospectively. A change from LIFO to FIFO is a change in principle requiring retrospective application, a mathematical error is corrected by restatement (prior period adjustment), and changing the subsidiaries in a consolidated group is a change in reporting entity that is applied retrospectively to all periods presented.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却方法の変更は「会計方針の変更によってもたらされる見積りの変更」（ASC 250-10-45-18）であり、方針の変更と見積りの変更が区別不能なため将来に向かって処理する。LIFOからFIFOへの変更は遡及適用が必要な方針の変更、前期の減価償却計算の計算ミスは修正再表示（prior period adjustment）で訂正すべき誤謬、連結対象子会社の構成変更は報告企業の変更であり表示全期間に遡及適用する。よって将来的処理となるのは減価償却方法の変更のみである。

questions.subtopic: Change in Depreciation Method - SL to Units of Production
questions.stem: Marwood Co. purchased a machine on January 1, Year 1, for $800,000 with a $50,000 salvage value and a 10-year useful life, depreciated straight-line. At the beginning of Year 3, Marwood changed to the units-of-production method, estimating 600,000 units of remaining capacity. The machine produced 90,000 units in Year 3. What is depreciation expense for Year 3?
questions.explanation: A change in depreciation method is accounted for prospectively as a change in estimate effected by a change in principle. Depreciation taken through Year 2 was [($800,000 − $50,000) ÷ 10] × 2 = $150,000, so the carrying amount is $650,000. The remaining depreciable base is $650,000 − $50,000 salvage = $600,000, or $1.00 per unit over 600,000 units. Year 3 expense is 90,000 × $1.00 = $90,000. Continuing the old method gives $75,000; failing to deduct salvage from the carrying amount gives ($650,000 ÷ 600,000) × 90,000 = $97,500; and using original cost less salvage without prior depreciation gives ($750,000 ÷ 600,000) × 90,000 = $112,500.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却方法の変更は「方針の変更によってもたらされる見積りの変更」として将来に向かって処理する（ASC 250-10-45-18）。Year 2末までの償却累計額は [($800,000 − $50,000) ÷ 10年] × 2年 = $150,000、帳簿価額は $650,000。残存償却基礎は $650,000 − 残存価額$50,000 = $600,000 で、残存生産能力600,000単位あたり$1.00。Year 3の償却費は 90,000単位 × $1.00 = $90,000。$75,000は旧定額法の継続、$97,500は残存価額の控除漏れ ($650,000 ÷ 600,000 × 90,000)、$112,500は既償却分を無視した計算 ($750,000 ÷ 600,000 × 90,000) による誤り。

questions.subtopic: LIFO to FIFO - Restated Comparative Net Income
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Ashbury Corp. changed from LIFO to FIFO. Year 2 net income as reported under LIFO was $300,000. FIFO inventory exceeded LIFO inventory by $80,000 at December 31, Year 1, and by $110,000 at December 31, Year 2. Ashbury's tax rate is 25%. In the comparative statements presented with Year 3, what is restated Year 2 net income?
questions.explanation: Under FIFO, Year 2 pretax income increases by the change in the inventory difference during Year 2: $110,000 − $80,000 = $30,000 (beginning inventory is $80,000 higher AND ending inventory is $110,000 higher, so cost of goods sold falls by the $30,000 net). After tax: $30,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $22,500, giving restated net income of $300,000 + $22,500 = $322,500. The $330,000 figure omits the tax effect on the $30,000; $382,500 incorrectly adds the full $110,000 ending difference (after tax) rather than the change during the year; and $300,000 ignores the restatement entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 遡及適用によりYear 2をFIFOで修正再表示する。Year 2の税引前利益への影響は、年間の棚卸差額の増分 $110,000 − $80,000 = $30,000（期首棚卸資産が$80,000多く、期末が$110,000多いため、売上原価が純額$30,000減少）。税効果考慮後は $30,000 × (1 − 25%) = $22,500 の増加で、修正後Year 2純利益は $300,000 + $22,500 = $322,500。$330,000は税効果の考慮漏れ、$382,500は年間増分でなく期末差額$110,000全額（税引後）を加算した誤り、$300,000は修正再表示を行っていない金額である。

questions.stem: In December of Year 1, before the Year 1 financial statements are issued, Fairfield Co.'s controller discovers that depreciation expense recorded in the first quarter of Year 1 was calculated incorrectly. How should this misstatement be handled?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Report it as a prior period adjustment to beginning Year 1 retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the previously issued Year 0 annual financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: Correct the depreciation amounts in the Year 1 financial statements before they are issued; no prior period adjustment is needed

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the error in the notes and correct it in Year 2

questions.explanation: The error arose and was discovered within the same fiscal year, before the annual statements were issued. The company simply records the correct depreciation in the Year 1 statements; there is no misstatement of any previously issued annual financial statements, so no prior period adjustment or restatement is required. A prior period adjustment applies only to errors in prior-year issued statements, Year 0 statements were not affected, and deferring the fix to Year 2 would knowingly issue misstated Year 1 statements.
questions.explanationJa: 誤謬は同一会計年度内に発生し、年度財務諸表の発行前に発見されている。この場合はYear 1の財務諸表上で正しい減価償却費に修正するだけでよく、既発行の年度財務諸表に誤りはないためprior period adjustmentや修正再表示は不要である。prior period adjustmentは過年度の既発行財務諸表の誤謬に対する処理であり、Year 0の財務諸表は影響を受けていない。また、修正をYear 2に先送りすることは誤った財務諸表を故意に発行することになり認められない。

questions.subtopic: Definition - What Is an Accounting Change
questions.stem: Under ASC 250, which of the following is NOT classified as an accounting change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in accounting estimate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Correction of an error in previously issued financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in accounting principle

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in reporting entity

questions.explanation: ASC 250 defines exactly three types of accounting changes: a change in accounting principle, a change in accounting estimate, and a change in reporting entity. The correction of an error in previously issued financial statements is specifically excluded from the definition of an accounting change — it is an error correction, accounted for by restatement (prior period adjustment), and the distinction matters because error corrections are never treated as discretionary changes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-20の定義上、会計上の変更（accounting change）は (1)会計方針の変更、(2)会計上の見積りの変更、(3)報告企業の変更 の3類型のみである。既発行財務諸表における誤謬の訂正は会計上の変更には含まれず、誤謬の訂正（error correction）として修正再表示（prior period adjustment）により処理される。この区別は重要で、誤謬訂正は経営者の裁量による「変更」ではなく、過去の誤りの是正である。

questions.subtopic: Retrospective Application - Restated Beginning Retained Earnings
questions.stem: Kendrick Co. changed from average cost to FIFO on January 1, Year 3. Retained earnings at January 1, Year 3, as previously reported under average cost was $850,000. Pretax income would have been higher under FIFO by $35,000 in Year 1 and $48,000 in Year 2. Kendrick's tax rate is 20%. What is the restated January 1, Year 3 retained earnings balance?
questions.explanation: The cumulative pretax effect through January 1, Year 3 is $35,000 + $48,000 = $83,000 of additional income under FIFO. Net of tax: $83,000 × (1 − 0.20) = $66,400. Restated retained earnings = $850,000 + $66,400 = $916,400. The $933,000 figure adds the gross $83,000 without the tax effect, $888,400 includes only the Year 2 effect net of tax ($48,000 × 0.80 = $38,400), and $878,000 includes only the Year 1 effect net of tax ($35,000 × 0.80 = $28,000).
questions.explanationJa: 遡及適用により、Year 3期首までの累積的影響額を期首利益剰余金に反映する（ASC 250-10-45-5）。税引前の累積影響は $35,000 + $48,000 = $83,000（FIFOの方が利益が大きい）。税効果考慮後は $83,000 × (1 − 20%) = $66,400。修正後期首利益剰余金 = $850,000 + $66,400 = $916,400。$933,000は税効果を無視して総額$83,000を加算した誤り、$888,400はYear 2分のみ（$48,000 × 80% = $38,400）、$878,000はYear 1分のみ（$35,000 × 80% = $28,000）を反映した誤りである。

questions.stem: Glenview Co. sponsors a defined benefit pension plan. At the beginning of the current year, Glenview's actuary revised the discount rate used to measure the projected benefit obligation based on current market conditions. Under ASC 250, this revision is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in accounting principle requiring retrospective application

choices.label: B
choices.text: An error correction requiring restatement of prior-year financial statements

choices.label: C
choices.text: A change in reporting entity

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in accounting estimate accounted for in the current and future periods

questions.explanation: Actuarial assumptions — discount rates, mortality, compensation growth — are estimates. Revising them based on new information or changed conditions is a change in accounting estimate, accounted for prospectively in the period of change and future periods. It is not a change in principle because the measurement method (the accounting principle for pensions) is unchanged; it is not an error because the prior rate was reasonable based on information available at the time; and it has nothing to do with the composition of the reporting entity.
questions.explanationJa: 割引率・死亡率・昇給率等の数理計算上の仮定は会計上の見積りである。市場環境の変化等の新情報に基づくこれらの見直しは見積りの変更に該当し、ASC 250-10-45-17により変更期および将来の期間で将来的に処理する。年金の測定方法（会計方針）自体は変わっていないため方針の変更ではなく、従前の割引率も当時入手可能な情報に基づき合理的であったため誤謬でもない。報告企業の構成にも無関係である。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Stanton Co. sold equipment for $95,000 cash. The equipment had a cost of $200,000 and accumulated depreciation of $130,000 (carrying amount $70,000) at the date of sale. The bookkeeper credited the entire $95,000 to miscellaneous revenue and did not remove the equipment's cost or accumulated depreciation from the books. Ignoring any depreciation after the sale date, Year 1 net income is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $95,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $70,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $25,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $25,000

questions.explanation: The correct accounting would recognize a gain of $95,000 − $70,000 = $25,000. Instead, the company recognized $95,000 of revenue without removing the $70,000 carrying amount, so income is overstated by $95,000 − $25,000 = $70,000 — exactly the carrying amount that should have been charged against the proceeds. The $95,000 figure ignores that a $25,000 gain was legitimately earned, $25,000 is the correct gain itself rather than the misstatement, and an understatement direction is impossible because too much income was recorded.
questions.explanationJa: 正しい処理では売却益 $95,000 − 帳簿価額$70,000 = $25,000 を認識すべきである。実際には帳簿価額$70,000を除却せずに$95,000全額を収益計上したため、利益は $95,000 − $25,000 = $70,000 過大、すなわち売却対価と相殺すべきだった帳簿価額の分だけ過大となる。$95,000は正当に稼得された$25,000の売却益を無視した金額、$25,000はあるべき売却益そのものであって過大計上額ではない。収益を過剰に計上しているため過小（understated）方向はあり得ない。

questions.subtopic: Modified Retrospective Adoption - Cumulative Effect Calculation
questions.stem: Beldon Corp. adopts a new FASB Accounting Standards Update on January 1, Year 1, that requires modified retrospective transition. At adoption, the new standard requires Beldon to recognize an additional liability of $250,000 with a corresponding cumulative-effect adjustment. Beldon's tax rate is 24%. What is the effect on Beldon's January 1, Year 1 retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $250,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $60,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $190,000 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect; the adjustment is recognized in Year 1 net income

questions.explanation: Under modified retrospective transition, the cumulative effect of applying the new standard is recognized as an adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the year of adoption, net of tax. The $250,000 pretax liability increase is offset by a $60,000 deferred tax benefit ($250,000 × 24%), so retained earnings decreases by $250,000 × (1 − 0.24) = $190,000. The gross $250,000 ignores the tax effect, $60,000 is only the tax effect, and running the cumulative effect through Year 1 income contradicts the modified retrospective method, which adjusts equity directly.
questions.explanationJa: 修正遡及（modified retrospective）移行では、新基準適用の累積的影響額を適用年度の期首利益剰余金で税効果考慮後の純額により調整する。負債$250,000の追加計上に対し繰延税金資産による便益 $250,000 × 24% = $60,000 が生じるため、利益剰余金の減少額は $250,000 × (1 − 24%) = $190,000。総額$250,000は税効果の考慮漏れ、$60,000は税効果部分のみ。累積的影響額をYear 1の純利益に計上する処理は、資本を直接修正する修正遡及法の趣旨に反する。

questions.stem: Rockwell Inc., a public company, retrospectively applies a voluntary change in accounting principle. Under ASC 250, which of the following disclosures is required for each prior period presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The effect of the change on income from continuing operations, net income, any other affected line items, and the related per-share amounts

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the cumulative effect on beginning retained earnings of the current year

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only a statement that the change was preferable, with no quantitative disclosures
