choices.label: D
choices.text: Projected effects of the change on the next five years of operations

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-50-1 requires, for each prior period retrospectively adjusted, disclosure of the effect of the change on income from continuing operations, net income, any other affected financial statement line item, and any affected per-share amounts. Disclosing only the cumulative retained earnings effect omits the required period-by-period detail, a bare preferability assertion lacks the required quantitative information (the reason the new principle is preferable is required in addition to, not instead of, the amounts), and projections of future effects are not required by ASC 250.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-50-1は、遡及適用された各表示期間について、継続事業からの利益、当期純利益、影響を受けるその他の表示科目、および関連する1株当たり金額への影響の開示を要求する。期首利益剰余金への累積的影響のみの開示では期間別の詳細が欠落する。新方針が望ましい（preferable）理由の説明は定量的開示に加えて要求されるものであり、それだけでは不十分。将来5年間への影響の予測開示はASC 250の要求事項ではない。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Periods Before Earliest Presented
questions.stem: Caldera Co. presents comparative financial statements for Year 2 and Year 3. In Year 3, Caldera discovers a material error that affected only Year 1, which is not presented. How should the error be corrected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate the Year 2 income statement to absorb the Year 1 error

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the correction in Year 3 net income because Year 1 is not presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: No correction is necessary because the affected period is not presented

choices.label: D
choices.text: Adjust the opening balances of assets, liabilities, and retained earnings of Year 2, the earliest period presented

questions.explanation: When an error relates to a period before the earliest period presented, restatement is accomplished by adjusting the opening balances of assets, liabilities, and retained earnings of the earliest period presented (Year 2). Forcing the error into the Year 2 income statement would misstate Year 2 results, recognizing it in Year 3 income would misstate the current period, and ignoring the error would leave the cumulative balances misstated even though the originating period is not displayed.
questions.explanationJa: 誤謬が表示される最も早い期間より前の期間にのみ関係する場合、ASC 250-10-45-23(b)に従い、表示される最も早い期間（Year 2）の資産・負債および利益剰余金の期首残高を修正することで修正再表示を行う。Year 1の誤謬をYear 2の損益計算書に吸収させればYear 2の業績が歪み、Year 3の損益に計上すれば当期が歪む。また、発生期間が表示されていなくても累積残高は誤ったままであるため、訂正しないという選択は認められない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Life and Salvage Revised Together
questions.stem: Dunmore Co. purchased a machine on January 1, Year 1, for $600,000 with an estimated salvage value of $60,000 and a 10-year useful life, depreciated straight-line. At the beginning of Year 5, Dunmore revised the total useful life to 8 years and the salvage value to $24,000. What is depreciation expense for Year 5?
questions.explanation: Depreciation through Year 4 is [($600,000 − $60,000) ÷ 10] × 4 = $216,000, leaving a carrying amount of $384,000. With a total life of 8 years, 4 years remain. Year 5 expense = ($384,000 − $24,000) ÷ 4 = $90,000. The $54,000 figure continues the old rate; $96,000 omits the new salvage value ($384,000 ÷ 4); and $72,000 incorrectly applies the new assumptions to original cost over the full new life [($600,000 − $24,000) ÷ 8] without isolating the remaining carrying amount and remaining years.
questions.explanationJa: Year 4末までの償却累計額は [($600,000 − $60,000) ÷ 10年] × 4年 = $216,000、帳簿価額は $384,000。耐用年数合計8年のうち残りは4年。見積りの変更として将来的に処理し（ASC 250-10-45-17）、Year 5の償却費は ($384,000 − 新残存価額$24,000) ÷ 4年 = $90,000。$54,000は旧償却率の継続、$96,000は新残存価額の控除漏れ（$384,000 ÷ 4）、$72,000は帳簿価額・残存年数によらず取得原価と新前提を機械的に当てはめた誤り [($600,000 − $24,000) ÷ 8]。

questions.stem: During the current year, Tarleton Co. entered into derivative contracts for the first time and adopted hedge accounting policies for them. Under ASC 250, the initial adoption of an accounting principle for events or transactions occurring for the first time is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in accounting principle requiring retrospective application

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change in accounting estimate requiring prospective treatment and disclosure of the income effect

choices.label: C
choices.text: Not a change in accounting principle; no retrospective application or accounting-change disclosure is required

choices.label: D
choices.text: An error correction requiring a prior period adjustment

questions.explanation: ASC 250 specifies that the initial adoption of an accounting principle for events or transactions occurring for the first time — or for those that were previously immaterial — is not a change in accounting principle. There is nothing to apply retrospectively because the transactions did not exist in prior periods. It is likewise not an estimate change (no prior estimate existed) and not an error (prior statements were not misstated).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-1により、初めて発生した事象・取引（または従来重要性が乏しかった取引）に対する会計方針の新規採用は、会計方針の変更には該当しない。過年度に当該取引が存在しなかった以上、遡及適用すべき対象が存在しないためである。同様に、過去の見積りが存在しないため見積りの変更でもなく、過年度の財務諸表に誤りがあったわけでもないため誤謬の訂正でもない。

questions.stem: Two errors occurred at Hollister Co. in Year 1: (1) a $20,000 merchandise purchase received in December Year 1 was not recorded until Year 2, even though the goods were on hand and were included in the Year 1 ending physical inventory count; and (2) separately, Year 1 ending inventory was understated by $35,000 due to a counting error on other goods. Considering both errors, Year 1 net income is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Understated by $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $55,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $55,000

questions.explanation: Cost of goods sold = beginning inventory + purchases − ending inventory. Error (1): the $20,000 purchase was omitted but the goods were included in the ending count, so purchases are understated by $20,000, which understates COGS and overstates income by $20,000. Error (2): ending inventory understated by $35,000 overstates COGS and understates income by $35,000. Net effect: income understated by $35,000 − $20,000 = $15,000. The overstatement direction reverses the larger error, and $55,000 incorrectly adds the two effects instead of offsetting them.
questions.explanationJa: 売上原価 = 期首棚卸資産 + 仕入 − 期末棚卸資産。誤謬(1): $20,000の仕入が未記帳である一方、商品自体は期末実地棚卸に含まれているため、仕入が$20,000過小 → 売上原価が過小 → 利益が$20,000過大。誤謬(2): 期末棚卸資産の$35,000過小計上により売上原価が過大 → 利益が$35,000過小。両者を合算すると、Year 1純利益は $35,000 − $20,000 = $15,000 の過小となる。過大方向の選択は大きい方の誤謬の影響を逆に捉えたもの、$55,000は相殺すべき2つの影響を加算した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Unearned Rent Recorded as Revenue
questions.stem: On July 1, Year 2, Belgrave Co. received $96,000 for a two-year lease of office space covering July 1, Year 2, through June 30, Year 4, and credited the entire amount to rent revenue. The error was discovered early in Year 3, after the Year 2 financial statements were issued. Belgrave's tax rate is 25%. What is the net-of-tax prior period adjustment to beginning Year 3 retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $72,000 decrease

choices.label: B
choices.text: $96,000 decrease

choices.label: C
choices.text: $18,000 decrease

choices.label: D
choices.text: $54,000 decrease

questions.explanation: By December 31, Year 2, only 6 of the 24 lease months had elapsed, so earned revenue is $96,000 × 6/24 = $24,000 and the remaining $72,000 should have been deferred as unearned rent. Year 2 revenue was therefore overstated by $72,000 pretax; net of tax, retained earnings is overstated by $72,000 × (1 − 0.25) = $54,000, and the prior period adjustment decreases beginning Year 3 retained earnings by $54,000. The $72,000 figure ignores the tax effect, $96,000 ignores both the earned portion and tax, and $18,000 is only the tax effect on the misstatement.
questions.explanationJa: Year 2末時点でリース期間24か月のうち6か月のみ経過しているため、稼得済収益は $96,000 × 6/24 = $24,000、残額$72,000は前受収益として繰り延べるべきであった。Year 2の収益は税引前で$72,000過大であり、税効果考慮後の利益剰余金の過大額は $72,000 × (1 − 25%) = $54,000。ASC 250-10-45-23により、Year 3期首利益剰余金を$54,000減額するprior period adjustmentを行う。$72,000は税効果の考慮漏れ、$96,000は稼得済部分と税効果の両方を無視、$18,000は税効果部分のみの金額である。

questions.stem: A staff accountant proposes reporting the cumulative effect of a voluntary change in accounting principle as a separate line item in the current year's income statement, after income from continuing operations. Under ASC 250, this proposal is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Acceptable, because cumulative effects are always reported in current income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Not acceptable; ASC 250 eliminated income statement recognition of cumulative effects, requiring instead an adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: Acceptable only if the company presents single-year financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Acceptable only if the change is from LIFO to FIFO

questions.explanation: ASC 250 (which carried forward FAS 154) eliminated the former APB 20 approach of reporting a cumulative-effect adjustment in the income statement. Changes in accounting principle are applied retrospectively, with the cumulative effect on periods before those presented recorded as an adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented — never as a component of current-period income. This holds whether single-year or comparative statements are presented, and the inventory methods involved do not create an exception.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250（FAS 154を引き継いだ規定）は、旧APB 20が認めていた「累積的影響額を当期の損益計算書に独立項目として表示する方法」を廃止した。会計方針の変更は遡及適用し、表示期間より前の累積的影響額は表示される最も早い期間の期首利益剰余金の修正として計上する。累積的影響額が当期損益の構成要素になることはなく、単年度表示か比較表示かを問わず、また棚卸資産の評価方法の種類によっても例外は生じない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, retrospective application of a change in accounting principle requires which of the following? I. Adjusting the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities as of the beginning of the earliest period presented for the cumulative effect of the new principle. II. Recording an offsetting adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings (or other appropriate components of equity) for that period.

choices.label: A
choices.text: I only

choices.label: B
choices.text: II only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both I and II

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither I nor II

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-5 prescribes both mechanics: the cumulative effect of applying the new principle to periods before the earliest period presented is reflected in the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities at the beginning of that earliest period, AND an offsetting adjustment is made to the opening balance of retained earnings (or other appropriate equity components, such as AOCI when applicable) for that same period. In addition, all periods presented are restated to reflect the period-specific effects of the new principle. Performing only one half of the entry would leave the balance sheet out of balance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-5は遡及適用の手続として両方を要求している。すなわち、(1)表示される最も早い期間より前の期間への累積的影響額を、当該期間の期首時点の資産・負債の帳簿価額に反映し、(2)同額の相手勘定として当該期間の期首利益剰余金（または該当する場合はAOCI等の他の資本項目）を修正する。さらに、表示される各期間の財務諸表は新方針による期間別影響額を反映して修正再表示する。どちらか一方のみでは貸借が一致しない。

questions.stem: Which of the following items should be reported as a prior period adjustment to beginning retained earnings in the current year's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The cumulative effect of revising the estimated useful life of a fleet of delivery vehicles

choices.label: B
choices.text: A mathematical mistake made in computing last year's depreciation expense, discovered this year

choices.label: C
choices.text: A loss on settlement of a lawsuit this year that arose from an event of a prior year for which no liability had been estimable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Additional warranty costs incurred this year on products sold last year, consistent with the original warranty estimate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250, a prior period adjustment is reserved for the correction of an error in previously issued financial statements. A mathematical mistake in computing a prior year's depreciation expense is an error, so its correction is recorded as an adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings (net of tax). Revising the estimated useful life of vehicles is a change in accounting estimate, which is accounted for prospectively in current and future periods. A lawsuit settlement arising from a prior-year event for which no reasonable estimate previously existed is the resolution of new information, treated as a current-period item, not an error. Warranty costs incurred consistent with the original estimate simply represent the normal use of an existing accrual and affect no prior period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250において、前期損益修正（prior period adjustment）は過年度に公表された財務諸表の誤謬の訂正に限定される。前年度の減価償却費の計算ミスは誤謬であり、その訂正は期首留保利益の修正（税効果考慮後）として処理する。車両の耐用年数の見直しは会計上の見積りの変更であり、当期以降にプロスペクティブに処理する。過年度の事象に起因する訴訟の当期和解損失は、従来合理的に見積れなかった新情報の解決であり、誤謬ではなく当期の損益項目となる。当初の保証見積りと整合する当期の保証費用は既存の引当金の通常の使用にすぎず、過年度には影響しない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Contract Cost Revision (Cumulative Catch-Up)
questions.stem: Drexel Construction Co. recognizes revenue over time on a $2,000,000 fixed-price contract using the cost-to-cost input method. The original estimate of total contract costs was $1,500,000. In Year 1, Drexel incurred $600,000 of costs and recognized gross profit accordingly. In Year 2, cumulative costs incurred reached $1,260,000 and Drexel revised its estimate of total contract costs to $1,800,000. What amount of gross profit (loss) should Drexel recognize in Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $140,000 gross profit

choices.label: B
choices.text: $60,000 gross profit

choices.label: D
choices.text: $200,000 gross profit

questions.explanation: A revision of estimated total contract costs is a change in accounting estimate, handled on a cumulative catch-up basis in the period of change. Year 1: $600,000 / $1,500,000 = 40% complete; estimated total gross profit was $2,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $500,000, so Year 1 gross profit = 40% × $500,000 = $200,000. Year 2: cumulative percentage = $1,260,000 / $1,800,000 = 70%; revised total gross profit = $2,000,000 − $1,800,000 = $200,000; cumulative gross profit to date = 70% × $200,000 = $140,000. Year 2 gross profit = $140,000 − $200,000 already recognized = $(60,000), a loss. The $140,000 figure is the cumulative amount and ignores the gross profit already recognized in Year 1. The $60,000 profit figure incorrectly applies only the 30% incremental completion to the revised total profit without the catch-up for the prior-year overstatement. The $200,000 figure is the revised total contract gross profit, not the Year 2 amount.
questions.explanationJa: 見積総原価の改訂は会計上の見積りの変更であり、変更期に累積キャッチアップ方式で処理する。Year 1: $600,000 ÷ $1,500,000 = 40%完成。当初見積総利益は $2,000,000 − $1,500,000 = $500,000 であり、Year 1の利益は 40% × $500,000 = $200,000。Year 2: 累積進捗率は $1,260,000 ÷ $1,800,000 = 70%。改訂後の総利益は $2,000,000 − $1,800,000 = $200,000 で、累積利益は 70% × $200,000 = $140,000。Year 2の利益は $140,000 − 既認識 $200,000 = $(60,000) の損失となる。$140,000 は累積額でありYear 1認識済利益を控除していない。$60,000 の利益は増分進捗30%を改訂後総利益に乗じただけで、前年の過大計上分のキャッチアップを反映していない。$200,000 は契約全体の改訂後総利益であり、Year 2の認識額ではない。

questions.stem: A material error affecting a prior year's net income is discovered in the current year, after the prior-year financial statements were issued. In the current year's financial statements, how is the correction of this error presented in the current year's income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is not included in current-year net income; it is reported as an adjustment, net of tax, to the opening balance of retained earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is reported as a separate component of current-year income from continuing operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is reported, net of tax, below income from continuing operations as a cumulative-effect item

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is included in current-year other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250, the correction of a material prior-period error is excluded entirely from the determination of current-period net income. It is reported as a prior period adjustment: the opening balance of retained earnings of the current period (or of the earliest period presented, if comparative statements are shown) is adjusted, net of the related tax effect, and prior-period statements presented are restated. Reporting it within income from continuing operations or as a cumulative-effect item in the income statement would distort current-period results; the cumulative-effect-in-income approach was eliminated for both changes in principle and error corrections. Other comprehensive income is limited to specifically designated items (such as certain unrealized gains and losses) and does not include error corrections.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250において、過年度の重要な誤謬の訂正は当期純利益の算定から完全に除外される。前期損益修正として、当期（比較財務諸表を表示する場合は表示最古年度）の期首留保利益を税効果考慮後で修正し、表示される過年度の財務諸表は修正再表示する。継続事業利益の内訳や損益計算書上の累積的影響額として表示すると当期業績を歪めるため認められず、損益計算書での累積的影響額表示というアプローチ自体が会計方針の変更・誤謬訂正の双方について廃止されている。その他の包括利益は特定の指定項目（一定の未実現損益等）に限定されており、誤謬の訂正は含まれない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Building Life Shortened
questions.stem: Corwin Co. purchased an office building on January 1, Year 1, for $900,000 with no salvage value, depreciating it on a straight-line basis over an estimated 30-year life. At the beginning of Year 11, after structural inspections, Corwin determined that the building's total useful life is 15 years from the acquisition date (salvage value remains zero). What amount of depreciation expense should Corwin recognize for Year 11?
questions.explanation: A revision of useful life is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively: the remaining book value is allocated over the remaining revised life. Depreciation for Years 1-10 was $900,000 / 30 = $30,000 per year, so accumulated depreciation at the beginning of Year 11 is $300,000 and book value is $600,000. The revised total life is 15 years, of which 10 have elapsed, leaving 5 years. Year 11 depreciation = $600,000 / 5 = $120,000. The $30,000 amount ignores the revision entirely. The $40,000 amount spreads the remaining book value over the full revised 15-year life instead of the 5 remaining years. The $60,000 amount divides original cost by the revised 15-year life, failing to use book value and remaining life.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数の見直しは会計上の見積りの変更であり、プロスペクティブに処理する。すなわち残存帳簿価額を改訂後の残存年数で配分する。Year 1〜10の減価償却は $900,000 ÷ 30 = 年 $30,000 で、Year 11期首の減価償却累計額は $300,000、帳簿価額は $600,000。改訂後の総耐用年数は15年で、既に10年経過しているため残存は5年。Year 11の減価償却費 = $600,000 ÷ 5 = $120,000。$30,000 は見直しを全く反映していない。$40,000 は残存帳簿価額を残存5年ではなく改訂後の全15年で配分した誤り。$60,000 は取得原価を改訂後15年で除しており、帳簿価額と残存年数を用いていない。

questions.stem: Halverson Co.'s Year 1 ending inventory was understated by $35,000 due to a counting error. The error was not detected until early Year 3, after the Year 2 books had been closed. Ignoring income taxes, what adjustment, if any, should Halverson make to retained earnings as of January 1, Year 3?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No adjustment, because the error has fully counterbalanced

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase retained earnings by $35,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease retained earnings by $35,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase retained earnings by $70,000

questions.explanation: An ending inventory error is counterbalancing: it reverses over two accounting periods. The Year 1 understatement of ending inventory overstated Year 1 cost of goods sold, understating Year 1 net income by $35,000. The same misstated amount became Year 2's understated beginning inventory, which understated Year 2 cost of goods sold and overstated Year 2 net income by $35,000. After Year 2 closes, the two effects offset exactly, so retained earnings at January 1, Year 3 is correctly stated and no adjusting entry is needed. A $35,000 increase or decrease would itself misstate retained earnings, and $70,000 incorrectly compounds the two annual effects instead of offsetting them. If comparative Year 1 and Year 2 statements are reissued, however, each year's net income must still be restated.
questions.explanationJa: 期末棚卸資産の誤謬はカウンターバランシング・エラーであり、2会計期間で自動的に解消する。Year 1期末棚卸資産の $35,000 過小計上はYear 1の売上原価を過大にし、Year 1純利益を $35,000 過小にした。同じ誤りがYear 2の期首棚卸資産の過小計上となり、Year 2の売上原価を過小、純利益を $35,000 過大にした。Year 2の決算後には両者が完全に相殺されるため、Year 3期首の留保利益は正しい金額であり修正仕訳は不要である。$35,000 の増額や減額はかえって留保利益を歪め、$70,000 は相殺すべき2年分の影響を合算した誤りである。ただし、Year 1・Year 2の比較財務諸表を再表示する場合は各年度の純利益の修正再表示が必要である。

questions.subtopic: Consecutive Accrual Omissions - Net Income Effect
questions.stem: Pelham Co. failed to accrue salaries payable of $25,000 at December 31, Year 1, and failed to accrue salaries payable of $40,000 at December 31, Year 2. The Year 1 unaccrued salaries were paid and expensed in January Year 2, and the Year 2 unaccrued salaries were paid and expensed in January Year 3. Ignoring income taxes, Year 2 net income was:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $40,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Overstated by $65,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $25,000

questions.explanation: Two offsetting effects hit Year 2. First, the omitted Year 2 year-end accrual of $40,000 understates Year 2 salaries expense, overstating Year 2 net income by $40,000. Second, the $25,000 of Year 1 salaries that should have been expensed in Year 1 was instead expensed when paid in Year 2, overstating Year 2 expense and understating Year 2 net income by $25,000. Net effect: $40,000 overstatement − $25,000 understatement = $15,000 overstatement of Year 2 net income. The $40,000 figure considers only the current-year omission and ignores the prior-year accrual flowing through Year 2 expense. The $65,000 figure adds the two effects instead of netting them. The $25,000 understatement reflects only the Year 1 carryover effect.
questions.explanationJa: Year 2には相殺し合う2つの影響がある。第一に、Year 2期末の未払給与 $40,000 の計上漏れにより、Year 2の給与費用が過小となり純利益が $40,000 過大になる。第二に、本来Year 1に費用計上すべきだった $25,000 がYear 2の支払時に費用処理されたため、Year 2の費用が過大となり純利益が $25,000 過小になる。正味の影響: 過大 $40,000 − 過小 $25,000 = Year 2純利益は $15,000 の過大計上。$40,000 は当年度の計上漏れのみを考慮し、前年度分がYear 2費用に流入した影響を無視している。$65,000 は相殺すべき2つの影響を合算した誤り。$25,000 過小はYear 1からの繰越影響のみを見た誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Change in Annual Goodwill Impairment Testing Date
questions.stem: Verano Corp., a calendar-year public company, has historically performed its annual goodwill impairment test as of October 1. Management wishes to move the annual testing date to December 1 to better align with its budgeting cycle, and has concluded the change will not delay, accelerate, or avoid an impairment charge. How should this change be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a correction of an error, with restatement of prior-period financial statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a change in accounting estimate, with cumulative catch-up recognized in current earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a change in the method of applying an accounting principle, requiring a preferability assessment but applied prospectively without retrospective adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is prohibited; once selected, the annual goodwill testing date cannot be changed

questions.explanation: ASC 350 permits the annual goodwill impairment test to be performed at any time during the year, provided it is performed at the same time every year. Changing that date is viewed as a change in the method of applying an accounting principle under ASC 250, so the entity (and, for SEC registrants, its auditor through a preferability letter) must conclude the new date is preferable. However, because retrospectively applying a new testing date to prior periods would require hindsight-laden valuations, the change is applied prospectively; no restatement or cumulative catch-up is recorded as long as the change does not delay, accelerate, or avoid an impairment. Treating it as an error is wrong because the prior October 1 date was an acceptable policy. Treating it as a pure estimate change with a catch-up misses the policy nature of the testing date. A prohibition on ever changing the date does not exist in the guidance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350は、毎年同じ時期に実施することを条件に、のれんの年次減損テストを年度中の任意の時点で行うことを認めている。テスト基準日の変更はASC 250上「会計方針の適用方法の変更」とみなされるため、新しい基準日が望ましい（preferable）との結論が必要であり、SEC登録企業では監査人のpreferability letterも求められる。ただし、過去の期間に新基準日を遡及適用することは事後的知見に基づく評価を要するため、変更が減損の遅延・加速・回避をもたらさない限りプロスペクティブに適用され、修正再表示や累積的影響額の計上は行わない。従来の10月1日基準日も容認される方針であったため誤謬には該当しない。テスト基準日は方針的性質を持つため、キャッチアップを伴う純粋な見積りの変更として扱うのも誤りである。基準日の変更を一切禁止する規定は存在しない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Estimate - Salvage Value Exceeds Book Value
questions.stem: Linden Co. purchased equipment on January 1, Year 1, for $150,000 with no salvage value and a 10-year useful life, using straight-line depreciation. At the beginning of Year 9, when accumulated depreciation was $120,000, Linden revised the estimated salvage value to $36,000 and the remaining useful life to 4 more years. What amount of depreciation expense should Linden recognize for Year 9?
questions.explanation: At the beginning of Year 9, book value is $150,000 − $120,000 = $30,000. The depreciable base under the revised estimates is book value minus revised salvage value: $30,000 − $36,000, which is negative. Because the revised salvage value exceeds the remaining book value, there is nothing left to depreciate, so Year 9 depreciation expense is $0. The asset is not written up to the higher salvage value; under GAAP no gain or appreciation is recorded for a favorable estimate revision—depreciation simply ceases until facts change. The $7,500 amount spreads the $30,000 book value over 4 years and ignores the salvage value. The $15,000 amount continues the original annual charge. The $9,000 amount incorrectly divides the salvage value itself over the remaining life.
questions.explanationJa: Year 9期首の帳簿価額は $150,000 − $120,000 = $30,000。改訂後の見積りによる償却基礎は帳簿価額から改訂後残存価額を控除した $30,000 − $36,000 でマイナスとなる。残存価額が帳簿価額を上回るため償却すべき金額は残っておらず、Year 9の減価償却費は $0 である。なお、資産を残存価額まで増額評価することはせず、有利な見積り改訂について利得や評価益を計上することはGAAP上認められない。事実が変化するまで減価償却を停止するだけである。$7,500 は残存価額を無視して帳簿価額 $30,000 を4年で配分した誤り。$15,000 は従来の年間償却額を継続した誤り。$9,000 は残存価額自体を残存年数で除した誤りである。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, the term 'restatement' is properly used to describe:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The application of a different accounting principle to prior periods as if that principle had always been used

choices.label: B
choices.text: The prospective recognition of the effect of a change in accounting estimate

choices.label: C
choices.text: The recasting of prior-period financial statements for a change in reporting entity

choices.label: D
choices.text: The revision of previously issued financial statements to correct an error

questions.explanation: ASC 250 deliberately reserves distinct terms for distinct events. 'Restatement' means revising previously issued financial statements to reflect the correction of an error. Applying a different accounting principle to prior periods as if it had always been used is 'retrospective application,' the term for voluntary changes in accounting principle. A change in accounting estimate is recognized prospectively and involves neither restatement nor retrospective application. A change in reporting entity is applied retrospectively to all periods presented, but it is described as retrospective application to the new entity's statements, not as a restatement. The distinction matters because describing financial statements as 'restated' signals to users that an error occurred.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250は事象ごとに用語を厳密に使い分けている。「restatement（修正再表示）」とは、誤謬の訂正を反映するために過年度に公表済みの財務諸表を修正することを指す。新しい会計方針を過年度から常に適用していたかのように適用することは「retrospective application（遡及適用）」であり、自発的な会計方針の変更に用いる用語である。会計上の見積りの変更はプロスペクティブに認識され、修正再表示も遡及適用も伴わない。報告主体の変更は表示される全期間に遡及して適用されるが、これは新しい報告主体の財務諸表への遡及適用であり、修正再表示とは呼ばない。「restated」という表現は誤謬の発生を利用者に伝えるシグナルであるため、この用語の区別は重要である。

questions.subtopic: Change to Equity Method - Significant Influence Obtained
questions.stem: Marbury Co. has held a 10% investment in Quill Inc. for several years, measured at fair value with changes in net income. On April 1 of the current year, Marbury acquired an additional 15% interest, raising its ownership to 25% and obtaining significant influence. How should Marbury account for the transition to the equity method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively adjust prior periods as if the equity method had been applied since the original 10% investment was acquired

choices.label: B
choices.text: Add the cost of the additional interest to the current basis of the existing interest and apply the equity method prospectively from April 1

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restate beginning retained earnings of the current year for the cumulative difference between fair value changes and equity-method earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Remeasure the entire investment to fair value through net income and then apply the equity method to the new basis

questions.explanation: Under ASC 323 (as amended by ASU 2016-07), when an investment qualifies for the equity method because of an increase in ownership, the investor adds the cost of the additional interest to the existing carrying amount of the previously held interest and adopts the equity method as of the date significant influence is obtained—no retroactive adjustment is made. Retrospective restatement of prior periods, which was required under the pre-2016 guidance, has been eliminated. Adjusting beginning retained earnings would treat the transition like an error correction or a retrospective principle change, which it is not. Remeasuring the entire investment through net income is the model for obtaining control in a business combination achieved in stages, not for attaining significant influence; the previously held interest's existing carrying amount (here, its fair value, since it was a fair-value-measured security) simply becomes part of the equity-method basis.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 323（ASU 2016-07による改正後）では、持分の追加取得により持分法の適用要件を満たした場合、追加取得した持分の取得原価を従来保有持分の帳簿価額に加算し、重要な影響力を獲得した日からプロスペクティブに持分法を適用する。遡及的な調整は行わない。2016年改正前に要求されていた過年度への遡及修正再表示は廃止された。期首留保利益の修正は、この移行を誤謬訂正や遡及的な方針変更のように扱うことになり不適切である。投資全体を公正価値で再測定して純利益に計上するのは段階取得により支配を獲得する企業結合のモデルであり、重要な影響力の獲得には適用されない。従来保有持分の既存の帳簿価額（本問では公正価値測定の証券であったためその公正価値）がそのまま持分法の基礎の一部となる。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Error - Purchase Recorded, Goods Excluded from Count
questions.stem: On December 28, Year 1, Sablewood Co. purchased merchandise costing $52,000, shipped FOB shipping point. Sablewood properly recorded the purchase and the related accounts payable in Year 1, but the goods were still in transit at December 31 and were not included in the year-end physical inventory count. As a result, Year 1 net income was:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $52,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Not affected

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $52,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated by $104,000

questions.explanation: Because the goods were shipped FOB shipping point, title passed to Sablewood when the goods left the seller, so they belong in Sablewood's December 31 inventory even though in transit. The purchase was correctly recorded, so purchases and accounts payable are properly stated. The only misstatement is ending inventory, which is understated by $52,000. In the cost of goods sold formula (beginning inventory + purchases − ending inventory), an understated ending inventory overstates cost of goods sold by $52,000, which understates net income by $52,000. An overstatement of income would occur only if ending inventory were overstated. A $104,000 effect would require both the count omission and an unrecorded purchase, but the purchase here was recorded; when both the purchase and the inventory are omitted, the two errors offset and income is unaffected.
questions.explanationJa: FOB shipping point（出荷地渡し）のため、商品が売主を離れた時点で所有権はSablewoodに移転しており、輸送中であっても12月31日時点の棚卸資産に含めるべきである。仕入は正しく記録されているため、仕入高と買掛金は適正である。誤りは期末棚卸資産の $52,000 過小計上のみである。売上原価の算式（期首棚卸資産＋仕入−期末棚卸資産）において、期末棚卸資産の過小計上は売上原価を $52,000 過大にし、純利益を $52,000 過小にする。利益の過大計上が生じるのは期末棚卸資産が過大な場合である。$104,000 の影響が生じるには棚卸の漏れに加え仕入の未記録が必要だが、本問では仕入は記録済みである。仕入と棚卸資産の両方が漏れた場合は2つの誤りが相殺し利益への影響はない。

questions.subtopic: Interim Reporting - Revision of Annual Effective Tax Rate
questions.stem: In the second quarter of the current year, Tolland Corp. revises its estimated annual effective income tax rate from 24% to 28% based on updated projections of full-year ordinary income and available credits. How should Tolland reflect this revision in its interim financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restate the first-quarter financial statements to apply the 28% rate to first-quarter income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Apply the 28% rate only to income earned in the second and subsequent quarters, leaving the first quarter at 24%

choices.label: C
choices.text: Report the cumulative difference as an adjustment to beginning retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Apply the 28% rate to year-to-date income, recognizing the entire cumulative adjustment in the second quarter's tax provision

questions.explanation: Under ASC 740-270, an entity computes its interim tax provision by applying the estimated annual effective tax rate to year-to-date ordinary income, and the revision of that rate is a change in accounting estimate. The second-quarter provision equals the new 28% rate applied to cumulative year-to-date income, minus the tax expense recognized in the first quarter, so the full catch-up effect of the rate change lands in the second quarter. Restating the first quarter is prohibited because estimate changes are never applied by restating previously issued statements. Applying the new rate only to income earned after the change fails the year-to-date cumulative model. An adjustment to beginning retained earnings is the treatment for error corrections, not for revisions of estimates.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 740-270では、期中の税金費用は見積年間実効税率を期首からの累計利益に適用して計算し、この税率の改訂は会計上の見積りの変更に該当する。第2四半期の税金費用は、新しい28%を累計利益に適用した金額から第1四半期に計上済みの税金費用を控除した額となり、税率変更のキャッチアップ効果は全額第2四半期に計上される。見積りの変更は公表済み財務諸表の修正再表示によって適用してはならないため、第1四半期の修正再表示は禁止される。変更後の期間の利益のみに新税率を適用する方法は累計ベースのモデルに反する。期首留保利益の修正は誤謬の訂正の処理であり、見積りの改訂には用いない。

questions.stem: Welby Corp. voluntarily changes an accounting principle. It can determine the period-specific effects of the change for Year 3 and Year 4 (the two most recent years), but determining the effects on Year 2 and earlier years is impracticable, although the cumulative effect at the beginning of Year 3 can be determined. Welby presents three years of comparative statements (Years 2-4). Under ASC 250, Welby should:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Apply the new principle to Years 3 and 4 and record the cumulative effect of earlier years as an adjustment to the beginning balances of Year 3, the earliest date practicable

choices.label: B
choices.text: Abandon the change, because retrospective application to every period presented is a precondition for any change in principle

choices.label: C
choices.text: Apply the new principle prospectively from the beginning of Year 4 only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the entire cumulative effect in Year 4 net income

questions.explanation: ASC 250 contains a tiered impracticability framework. When it is impracticable to determine period-specific effects for some but not all prior periods, the entity applies the new principle as of the earliest date practicable and adjusts the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities, with an offsetting adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings, at the beginning of that period—here, Year 3. Year 2 remains on the old principle with disclosure. The change need not be abandoned; impracticability limits, but does not preclude, adoption. Full prospective application from the current period is reserved for the situation in which the cumulative effect at the beginning of any period cannot be determined at all (the classic example being a change to LIFO). Recognizing the cumulative effect in current net income reflects the superseded cumulative-effect approach eliminated by ASC 250.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250は実務上不可能（impracticable）の場合の段階的な枠組みを定めている。一部の過年度についてのみ期間別影響額の算定が実務上不可能な場合、実務上可能な最も古い日（本問ではYear 3期首）から新方針を適用し、その期首において資産・負債の帳簿価額を修正し、相手勘定として期首留保利益を調整する。Year 2は旧方針のまま表示し、その旨を開示する。変更を断念する必要はなく、実務上の不可能性は適用を制限するが妨げるものではない。当期からの完全なプロスペクティブ適用は、どの期間についても期首時点の累積的影響額が算定できない場合（典型例はLIFOへの変更）に限られる。累積的影響額を当期純利益に計上する方法はASC 250により廃止された旧アプローチである。

questions.subtopic: Error Correction - Depreciation Recorded on Land
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 1, Ravello Co. acquired land for $250,000 and erroneously began depreciating it at 5% per year on a straight-line basis. The error is discovered at the beginning of Year 4, before the Year 4 books are adjusted. Ignoring income taxes, the correcting entry should increase retained earnings by:
questions.explanation: Land is not a depreciable asset because it has an indefinite useful life, so every dollar of depreciation recorded on it is an error. Annual erroneous depreciation was $250,000 × 5% = $12,500, recorded for Years 1, 2, and 3, a total of $12,500 × 3 = $37,500. Because each year's depreciation expense overstated expenses and understated net income, retained earnings at the beginning of Year 4 is understated by $37,500. The correcting entry debits accumulated depreciation for $37,500 and credits retained earnings (as a prior period adjustment) for $37,500. The $12,500 amount reflects only one year of the error, $25,000 reflects only two years, and $50,000 assumes four years of depreciation even though Year 4 has not yet been recorded.
questions.explanationJa: 土地は耐用年数が無限であるため減価償却の対象とならず、計上された減価償却費は全額誤謬である。誤った年間償却費は $250,000 × 5% = $12,500 で、Year 1〜3の3年間で合計 $12,500 × 3 = $37,500。各年度で費用が過大・純利益が過小となっていたため、Year 4期首の留保利益は $37,500 過小である。修正仕訳は減価償却累計額 $37,500 を借方、前期損益修正として留保利益 $37,500 を貸方に計上する。$12,500 は1年分のみ、$25,000 は2年分のみを反映した誤りであり、$50,000 はまだ計上されていないYear 4分まで含めて4年分を仮定した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Completed-Contract to Percentage-of-Completion - Retained Earnings Adjustment
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Brockton Builders Inc. voluntarily changed from the completed-contract method to recognizing revenue over time (percentage-of-completion) for its long-term contracts, and its records are adequate for retrospective application. Cumulative gross profit through December 31, Year 2 would have been $240,000 under percentage-of-completion; cumulative gross profit actually recognized under the completed-contract method through that date was $90,000. Brockton's tax rate is 30%. The adjustment to the beginning balance of Year 3 retained earnings is an increase of:
questions.explanation: A voluntary change in accounting principle is applied retrospectively, with the cumulative effect on periods before those presented recorded as an adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings, net of tax. The pretax cumulative difference is $240,000 − $90,000 = $150,000 of additional gross profit that would have been recognized in earlier years under percentage-of-completion. The related tax effect is $150,000 × 30% = $45,000, recorded as a deferred tax liability. The net increase to beginning retained earnings is $150,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $105,000. The $150,000 figure omits the tax effect, $240,000 uses the full percentage-of-completion cumulative profit without subtracting the profit already recognized, and $45,000 is the tax effect itself rather than the net adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: 自発的な会計方針の変更は遡及適用し、表示期間より前の期間に係る累積的影響額は税効果考慮後で期首留保利益の修正として計上する。税引前の累積差額は $240,000 − $90,000 = $150,000 であり、これは工事進行基準であれば過年度に追加認識されていた利益である。税効果は $150,000 × 30% = $45,000 で、繰延税金負債として計上する。期首留保利益の正味増加額は $150,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $105,000。$150,000 は税効果を控除していない。$240,000 は既に認識済みの利益を差し引かずに工事進行基準の累積利益全額を用いた誤り。$45,000 は正味の修正額ではなく税効果そのものである。

questions.subtopic: Inventory Method Change - Effect on Restated Prior-Year Income
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Dunbar Co. changed from the weighted-average method to FIFO for inventory costing and will restate its Year 2 comparative statements. Inventory under each method was as follows: at December 31, Year 1, FIFO inventory exceeded weighted-average inventory by $30,000; at December 31, Year 2, FIFO inventory exceeded weighted-average inventory by $48,000. Ignoring income taxes, restated Year 2 net income under FIFO will differ from previously reported Year 2 net income by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: An increase of $48,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: A decrease of $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: An increase of $78,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: An increase of $18,000

questions.explanation: The effect of an inventory method change on a given year's income equals the change in the inventory difference during that year. Cost of goods sold = beginning inventory + purchases − ending inventory. Under FIFO, Year 2 beginning inventory is $30,000 higher (which increases COGS by $30,000) and Year 2 ending inventory is $48,000 higher (which decreases COGS by $48,000). Net effect: COGS is $18,000 lower under FIFO, so restated Year 2 net income is $18,000 higher. The $48,000 increase considers only the ending inventory difference, the $30,000 decrease considers only the beginning inventory difference (with its sign correct in isolation but incomplete), and $78,000 adds the two differences instead of netting them.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産評価方法の変更が特定年度の利益に与える影響は、その年度中の棚卸資産差額の変動に等しい。売上原価＝期首棚卸資産＋仕入−期末棚卸資産。FIFOではYear 2の期首棚卸資産が $30,000 大きく（売上原価を $30,000 増加させる）、期末棚卸資産が $48,000 大きい（売上原価を $48,000 減少させる）。正味の影響として売上原価はFIFOの方が $18,000 小さく、修正再表示後のYear 2純利益は $18,000 大きくなる。$48,000 の増加は期末差額のみ、$30,000 の減少は期首差額のみ（単独では方向は正しいが不完全）を考慮した誤りであり、$78,000 は相殺すべき2つの差額を合算した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Change from Direct Write-Off to Allowance Method
questions.stem: For several years, Granby Co. has recognized bad debt expense using the direct write-off method, even though uncollectible amounts have always been material and reasonably estimable. In the current year, Granby adopts the allowance method. How should this change be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a change in accounting estimate, applied prospectively

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a correction of an error, with restatement of prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a voluntary change in accounting principle, applied retrospectively because the allowance method is preferable

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a change in reporting entity, applied retrospectively to all periods presented

questions.explanation: A change in accounting principle, by definition, is a change from one generally accepted accounting principle to another generally accepted alternative. The direct write-off method is not GAAP when uncollectible accounts are material and estimable, because it fails to match credit losses with the related revenue and overstates receivables. A change from a principle that is not generally accepted to one that is generally accepted is therefore a correction of an error under ASC 250, requiring restatement of prior periods presented and a net-of-tax adjustment to the opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented. The estimate-change and principle-change treatments both presuppose that the prior method was acceptable, which it was not. A change in reporting entity involves a change in the composition of the entities being reported on and is unrelated to receivables measurement.
questions.explanationJa: 会計方針の変更とは、定義上、一般に認められた会計原則から別の一般に認められた代替的原則への変更をいう。回収不能額が重要かつ合理的に見積可能な場合、直接償却法は貸倒損失と関連収益の対応を欠き、債権を過大表示するためGAAPではない。したがって、GAAPでない方法からGAAPである方法への変更はASC 250上の誤謬の訂正に該当し、表示される過年度の修正再表示と、表示最古年度の期首留保利益の税効果考慮後の修正が必要となる。見積りの変更や方針の変更としての処理は、従来の方法が容認されていたことを前提とするが、本問では容認されていない。報告主体の変更は報告対象となる企業の構成の変更であり、債権の測定とは無関係である。

questions.subtopic: Prior-Year Misclassification Within the Income Statement
questions.stem: In Year 2, Holcomb Co. discovers that $300,000 of shipping and handling costs in Year 1 were misclassified as selling expenses when they should have been reported as cost of goods sold under Holcomb's accounting policy. Year 1 net income, retained earnings, and total assets were unaffected by the misclassification. Year 1 statements are presented comparatively with Year 2. The misclassification is material to the gross margin trend. How should Holcomb address this?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify the $300,000 in the comparative Year 1 income statement as an error correction; no adjustment to beginning retained earnings is needed

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record a $300,000 prior period adjustment, net of tax, to the beginning balance of Year 2 retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: No action is required because net income was correctly stated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the matter in the notes but leave the comparative Year 1 income statement unchanged

questions.explanation: An error includes misclassifications within the financial statements, not only misstatements of net income. Because the Year 1 income statement is presented comparatively and the misclassification is material to gross margin, the comparative Year 1 statement must be restated to move the $300,000 from selling expenses to cost of goods sold, with disclosure of the nature of the error. No adjustment to beginning retained earnings is needed because the error had no effect on net income or equity—the retained earnings adjustment mechanism exists for errors that misstated prior income. Doing nothing, or disclosing without revising the comparative presentation, would leave a materially misleading gross margin trend in the comparative statements. A net-of-tax retained earnings adjustment is inapplicable because there is no income effect to correct.
questions.explanationJa: 誤謬には純利益の虚偽表示だけでなく、財務諸表内の表示区分の誤り（misclassification）も含まれる。Year 1の損益計算書が比較表示され、この区分誤りが売上総利益率のトレンドにとって重要であるため、比較表示するYear 1の損益計算書を修正再表示し、$300,000 を販売費から売上原価へ振り替え、誤謬の内容を開示しなければならない。この誤謬は純利益にも資本にも影響しなかったため、期首留保利益の修正は不要である。留保利益の修正という仕組みは過年度の利益を歪めた誤謬のためのものである。何もしない、あるいは比較表示を修正せず注記のみで済ませることは、比較財務諸表に重要な誤解を招く売上総利益率のトレンドを残すことになる。税効果考慮後の留保利益修正は、訂正すべき損益への影響が存在しないため適用されない。

questions.subtopic: Combined Errors - Inventory and Accrual Effect on Retained Earnings
questions.stem: Two errors occurred at Wexford Co. during Year 2 and remain uncorrected: (1) ending inventory at December 31, Year 2 was overstated by $28,000, and (2) accrued interest expense of $12,000 incurred in Year 2 was not recorded until paid in Year 3. Ignoring income taxes, retained earnings at December 31, Year 2 was:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated by $16,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Overstated by $28,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated by $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated by $40,000

questions.explanation: Each error pushes Year 2 net income, and therefore year-end retained earnings, in the same direction. The $28,000 overstatement of ending inventory understates cost of goods sold, overstating net income by $28,000. The omission of $12,000 of accrued interest expense understates expenses, overstating net income by another $12,000. Combined, retained earnings at December 31, Year 2 is overstated by $28,000 + $12,000 = $40,000. The $16,000 figure incorrectly offsets the two effects against each other, even though both overstate income. The $28,000 figure ignores the omitted interest accrual. An understatement of $40,000 reverses the direction of both errors.
questions.explanationJa: 2つの誤謬はいずれもYear 2の純利益、ひいては期末留保利益を同じ方向に歪める。期末棚卸資産の $28,000 過大計上は売上原価を過小にし、純利益を $28,000 過大にする。未払利息費用 $12,000 の計上漏れは費用を過小にし、純利益をさらに $12,000 過大にする。合計で、Year 2期末の留保利益は $28,000 + $12,000 = $40,000 の過大計上となる。$16,000 は、両方とも利益を過大にする誤謬であるにもかかわらず2つの影響を相殺した誤りである。$28,000 は未払利息の計上漏れを無視している。$40,000 の過小計上は両誤謬の方向を逆にした誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Retrospective Inventory Change - Deferred Tax Entry
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, Galloway Corp. voluntarily changed its inventory method from LIFO to FIFO for both a class of inventory and its financial reporting, and retrospective application is practicable. FIFO inventory at January 1, Year 3 exceeds the LIFO carrying amount by $200,000. Galloway's enacted tax rate is 25%, and the temporary difference will reverse in future years. As part of recording the change as of January 1, Year 3, Galloway should recognize a deferred tax liability of:
questions.explanation: Retrospective application requires adjusting the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities at the beginning of Year 3, including the direct tax effects of the change. The entry increases inventory by $200,000. Because the financial-reporting basis of inventory now exceeds its tax basis (LIFO typically continues for tax under the conformity rules until tax accounting method change procedures are completed, and in any case the book write-up creates a taxable temporary difference), a deferred tax liability of $200,000 × 25% = $50,000 is recognized. The remaining $150,000 is credited to beginning retained earnings. A zero liability ignores the taxable temporary difference created by the higher book basis. The $200,000 amount is the gross inventory step-up, not its tax effect. The $150,000 amount is the net-of-tax credit to retained earnings, not the deferred tax liability.
questions.explanationJa: 遡及適用では、Year 3期首時点で資産・負債の帳簿価額を修正し、変更の直接的な税効果も計上する。仕訳では棚卸資産を $200,000 増額する。財務報告上の棚卸資産の簿価が税務上の基礎を上回ることになるため（税務上はLIFOコンフォーミティ・ルールにより税務会計方法の変更手続が完了するまでLIFOが継続するのが通常であり、いずれにせよ帳簿上の増額は将来加算一時差異を生む）、繰延税金負債 $200,000 × 25% = $50,000 を認識する。残額 $150,000 は期首留保利益の貸方に計上する。負債ゼロは簿価の増額が生む将来加算一時差異を無視している。$200,000 は棚卸資産の増額総額であり税効果ではない。$150,000 は税効果控除後の留保利益への貸方計上額であり、繰延税金負債ではない。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Prior Period Adjustment
questions.stem: How should the correction of a mathematical error in the calculation of prior years' depreciation be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a prior period adjustment.

choices.label: B
choices.text: As an adjustment to the current period's depreciation expense.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a change in accounting principle.

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a change in accounting estimate.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-23, the correction of an error in previously issued financial statements is reported as a prior period adjustment. A mathematical error in the calculation of depreciation constitutes an error correction, not a change in estimate or principle. The correction is recorded as a direct adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings of the earliest period presented, and prior period financial statements are restated.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-23に基づき、過去に発行された財務諸表の誤謬訂正は前期修正として報告されます。減価償却計算における数学的誤りは見積変更や会計方針変更ではなく誤謬訂正に該当します。訂正は表示される最初期の期首利益剰余金残高への直接調整として記録され、過年度財務諸表は修正再表示されます。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Inventory Error Effects
questions.stem: During 20X9, Steve Company discovered that the ending inventories reported on its financial statements were incorrect by the following amounts: 20X7 $60,000 understated 20X8 $75,000 overstated Steve uses the periodic inventory system to ascertain year-end quantities that are converted to dollar amounts using the FIFO cost method. Prior to any adjustments for these errors and ignoring income taxes, Steve's retained earnings at January 1, 20X9, would be

choices.label: A
choices.text: Correct.

choices.label: B
choices.text: $15,000 overstated.

choices.label: C
choices.text: $75,000 overstated.

choices.label: D
choices.text: $135,000 overstated.

questions.explanation: Inventory errors are self-correcting over two years. The 20X7 understatement of $60,000 reduces 20X7 net income by $60,000 (COGS overstated) but increases 20X8 net income by $60,000 (opening inventory understated, so 20X8 COGS understated). The net effect of the 20X7 error on cumulative retained earnings at January 1, 20X9, is zero. The 20X8 overstatement of $75,000 overstates 20X8 net income by $75,000 (COGS understated) and has not yet self-corrected because 20X9 beginning inventory carries the same overstatement. Therefore, retained earnings at January 1, 20X9, are overstated by $75,000.
questions.explanationJa: 棚卸資産の誤謬は2年間で自己修正されます。20X7年の$60,000過少計上は20X7年純利益を$60,000減少させ（売上原価過大）、20X8年純利益を$60,000増加させます（期首棚卸資産過少のため売上原価過小）。20X7年誤謬が2020年1月1日時点の累積利益剰余金に与える純影響はゼロです。20X8年の$75,000過大計上は20X8年純利益を$75,000過大にし（売上原価過小）、20X9年期首棚卸資産に同じ過大計上が引き継がれるため自己修正されていません。したがって、2020年1月1日時点の利益剰余金は$75,000過大となります。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Omitted Accrual Effects
questions.stem: During December Year 10, Nile Co. incurred special insurance costs but did not record these costs until payment was made during Year 11. These insurance costs related to inventory that had been sold by December 31, Year 10. What is the effect of the omission on Nile's accrued liabilities and retained earnings at December 31, Year 10? Accrued liabilities Retained earnings

choices.label: A
choices.text: No effect No effect

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect Understated

choices.label: C
choices.text: Understated Overstated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understated No effect

questions.explanation: The insurance costs incurred in December Year 10 should have been accrued as a liability at December 31, Year 10 (debit expense, credit accrued liabilities). Because the entry was not made, accrued liabilities at December 31, Year 10, are understated by the amount of the unrecorded liability. Since the expense was not recorded, cost of goods sold (or insurance expense) is understated, net income is overstated, and retained earnings at December 31, Year 10, are overstated.
questions.explanationJa: Year 10年12月に発生した保険コストは、2020年12月31日時点で負債として未払計上すべきでした（費用を借方、未払費用を貸方）。計上が行われなかったため、Year 10年12月31日時点の未払費用は計上漏れ額だけ過少となっています。費用が計上されなかったため、売上原価（または保険費用）は過少、純利益は過大となり、Year 10年12月31日時点の利益剰余金は過大になっています。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Restatement Requirements
questions.stem: Which of the following examples would require restatement of prior years' financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A calculation change of warranty obligations based on updated claim information for the prior year.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change from the income tax basis of accounting to the accrual basis.

choices.label: C
choices.text: An insurance premium that was due in the prior year but that lapsed because the policy was not paid.

choices.label: D
choices.text: An intangible asset with a remaining estimated amortization period of two years, which is determined to be obsolete.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-23, restatement of prior period financial statements is required when correcting errors. A change from the income tax basis (a non-GAAP basis) to the accrual basis (GAAP) constitutes a correction of an error requiring restatement. A change in warranty estimates based on new information is a change in accounting estimate applied prospectively. An insurance premium that lapsed is a current-period event, not an error in prior periods. Writing off an intangible determined to be obsolete is a change in estimate applied in the current period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-23に基づき、誤謬訂正の際には過年度財務諸表の修正再表示が必要です。税務基準（非GAAP）から発生主義（GAAP）への変更は誤謬訂正に該当し、修正再表示が求められます。新情報に基づく保証引当金の見積変更は会計上の見積変更であり将来に向けて適用されます。失効した保険料は当期の事象であり過年度の誤謬ではありません。陳腐化と判断された無形資産の償却は当期に適用される見積変更です。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Prior Period Adjustment Calculation
questions.stem: On January 2, Year 4, Raft Corp. discovered that it had incorrectly expensed a $210,000 machine purchased on January 2, Year 1. Raft estimated the machine's original useful life to be 10 years and its salvage value at $10,000. Raft uses the straight-line method of depreciation and is subject to a 30% tax rate. In its December 31, Year 4, financial statements, what amount should Raft report as a prior-period adjustment?
questions.explanation: Annual straight-line depreciation = ($210,000 − $10,000) ÷ 10 years = $20,000 per year. Over Years 1–3, correct total depreciation = 3 × $20,000 = $60,000. Because the machine was fully expensed in Year 1, the cumulative pre-tax understatement of retained earnings = $210,000 expensed − $60,000 correct depreciation = $150,000. The prior-period adjustment reported in December 31, Year 4, financial statements is the net-of-tax amount: $150,000 × (1 − 0.30) = $105,000. (The correcting journal entry debits Machinery $210,000, credits Accumulated Depreciation $60,000, establishes a Deferred Tax Liability of $45,000, and credits Retained Earnings — prior period adjustment — $105,000.)
questions.explanationJa: 定額法の年間減価償却費=($210,000-$10,000)÷10年=$20,000/年。Year 1〜3の正しい減価償却費合計=3×$20,000=$60,000。機械がYear 1に全額費用処理されたため、利益剰余金の累積税引前過少計上額=$210,000（誤って費用処理）-$60,000（正しい3年分減価償却）=$150,000。Year 4年12月31日財務諸表で報告される前期修正額は税効果考慮後：$150,000×(1-0.30)=$105,000。（訂正仕訳：機械$210,000借方、減価累計額$60,000貸方、繰延税金負債$45,000貸方、利益剰余金[前期修正]$105,000貸方。）

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Correcting Journal Entry
questions.stem: On January 2, Year 3, Althouse Co. discovered that it had incorrectly expensed equipment that was purchased on January 2, Year 1 for $150,000. The equipment should have been depreciated over five years with no salvage value. What amount, if any, should be adjusted to Althouse's depreciation expense on January 2, Year 3, when Althouse records the correcting entry?
questions.explanation: When correcting an error in previously issued financial statements, the effect on prior periods is recorded as a prior period adjustment directly to retained earnings (net of tax), not as depreciation expense in the current period. On January 2, Year 3, Althouse debits the Equipment asset account for $150,000, credits Accumulated Depreciation for $60,000 (2 years × $30,000/year), and credits Retained Earnings (prior period adjustment) for the remaining $90,000 net of tax effect. No amount is adjusted to the current-period depreciation expense line; the $60,000 goes to Accumulated Depreciation as part of the correcting entry.
questions.explanationJa: 過去の財務諸表の誤謬を訂正する際、過年度への影響は当期の減価償却費としてではなく、前期修正として直接利益剰余金（税効果考慮後）に記録されます。Year 3年1月2日に、Althouseは固定資産（機器）勘定に$150,000借方、減価累計額に$60,000（2年×$30,000/年）貸方、残額を前期修正として利益剰余金に貸方記入します。当期の減価償却費行に調整する金額はゼロであり、$60,000は訂正仕訳の一部として減価累計額に計上されます。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Change in Reporting Entity
questions.stem: When there is a change in the reporting entity, how should the change be reported in the financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospectively, including note disclosures.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospectively, including note disclosures, and application to all prior period financial statements presented.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Currently, including note disclosures.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Note disclosures only.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-21, a change in reporting entity (such as a change in the entities included in consolidated financial statements) is reported retrospectively by restating all prior period financial statements presented as if the new reporting entity had existed in all prior periods. This approach ensures comparability across periods. Required note disclosures include the nature of the change and the reason for it, as well as the effect on income from continuing operations, net income, and other comprehensive income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-21に基づき、報告実体の変更（連結財務諸表に含まれる企業の変更など）は、新しい報告実体が過去全期間に存在していたかのように、表示される全ての過年度財務諸表を修正再表示することで遡及的に報告されます。このアプローチにより期間比較可能性が確保されます。必要な注記開示には変更の性質・理由、継続事業からの利益・純利益・その他包括利益への影響が含まれます。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Retrospective Application
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 3, a company changed its inventory costing method from LIFO to FIFO. The company's Year 3 financial statements contain comparative information for Year 2. How should the company present the Year 1 effect of the change in accounting principle in its Year 3 comparative financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As an adjustment to the beginning Year 2 inventory balance with an offsetting adjustment to beginning Year 2 retained earnings.

choices.label: B
choices.text: As part of income from continuing operations in the Year 2 income statement.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a prospective change in Year 3 and forward with no adjustment in Year 2.

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a note disclosure only.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-5, a change in accounting principle is reported retrospectively by adjusting all prior periods presented. Since the Year 3 financial statements present comparative Year 2 data, the cumulative effect of the change for all periods before Year 2 (i.e., the Year 1 effect) is presented as an adjustment to the beginning Year 2 inventory balance with an offsetting adjustment to beginning Year 2 retained earnings. Year 2 itself is restated using FIFO throughout.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-5に基づき、会計方針の変更は表示される全ての過年度を調整することで遡及的に報告されます。Year 3の財務諸表にYear 2の比較情報が含まれているため、Year 2以前の全期間（つまりYear 1の影響）の累積効果は、Year 2期首棚卸資産残高への調整と、それに対応するYear 2期首利益剰余金への調整として表示されます。Year 2自体はFIFOを用いて全面的に修正再表示されます。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Revenue Recognition Method Change
questions.stem: Maas Co. manufactures heavy equipment to customer specifications on a contract basis. Accounting for these long-term contracts was switched from recognizing revenue at a point in time to recognizing revenue over a period of time. How should Maas. Co. classify and account for this change on its financial statements? Classification of the change Financial statement impact

choices.label: A
choices.text: Change in accounting estimate Prospective adjustment

choices.label: B
choices.text: Change in accounting estimate Retrospective adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Change in accounting principle Prospective adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Change in accounting principle Retrospective adjustment

questions.explanation: Switching the method of revenue recognition from point-in-time to over-time constitutes a change in accounting principle under ASC 250-10-20. Changes in accounting principle are generally reported retrospectively, meaning all prior period financial statements presented are restated as if the new principle had always been applied. This differs from a change in accounting estimate, which is applied prospectively. Therefore, the change is classified as a change in accounting principle requiring a retrospective adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: 収益認識の方法を一時点認識から一定期間にわたる認識に変更することは、ASC 250-10-20に基づく会計方針の変更に該当します。会計方針の変更は原則として遡及的に報告され、新方針が常に適用されていたかのように表示される全ての過年度財務諸表が修正再表示されます。これは将来に向けて適用される会計上の見積変更とは異なります。したがって、この変更は遡及的調整を要する会計方針の変更に分類されます。

questions.subtopic: Accounting Changes and Error Corrections - Self-Correcting Error Effects
questions.stem: At the end of Year 1, Ritzcar Co. failed to accrue production costs incurred during Year 1 but paid (and recorded) in Year 2. The error was not repeated in Year 2. What was the effect of this error on Year 1 ending working capital and on the Year 2 ending retained earnings balance? Year 1 ending working capital Year 2 ending retained earnings

choices.label: A
choices.text: Overstated Overstated

choices.label: B
choices.text: No effect Overstated

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect No effect

choices.label: D
choices.text: Overstated No effect

questions.explanation: By failing to accrue production costs in Year 1, Ritzcar omitted both the expense (understating Year 1 cost/COGS, overstating Year 1 net income and retained earnings) and the related current liability (accrued expenses payable). Since both a current asset is not affected and a current liability is not recorded, working capital (current assets minus current liabilities) is overstated because the omitted liability would have reduced working capital. In Year 2, the costs are paid and recorded, correcting the Year 1 understatement of expense and overstatement of income — so Year 2 income is understated by the same amount. By December 31, Year 2, these errors have fully offset each other, leaving Year 2 ending retained earnings correct (no effect).
questions.explanationJa: Year 1末に製造コストを未払計上しなかったため、Ritzcarはコスト（Year 1の売上原価過少→純利益・利益剰余金過大）と関連する流動負債（未払費用）の両方を省略しました。省略された負債は運転資本を減少させるはずでしたが、計上されなかったため、Year 1の期末運転資本（流動資産-流動負債）は過大となっています。Year 2では当該コストが支払・計上され、Year 1の費用過少と収益過大が修正されます——Year 2の収益が同額だけ過少計上されます。Year 2年12月31日時点でこれらの誤謬は完全に相殺され、Year 2期末利益剰余金は正確（影響なし）となります。
